title: choicer — System Architecture
text: restructure eliminates per-draw allocations splitting numerator four independently handled pieces: Identity C (linear pieces): buf_Pzz_cc/cd/dd buf_diff_HV_cc accumulate two terms HisH_{} linear PisP_{} via direct scalar-times-matrix additions, avoiding dense H_is assembly. Identities + B (outer-product pieces): G_stash F_stash (n_params×Sn\_\text{params} \times S) filled column--column draw loop. loop single BLAS level-3 multiply [G|F][G|F]ᵀ delivers GG⊤+FF⊤=∑sPis(gisgis⊤+sum_Pzissum_Pzis⊤)G G^\top + F F^\top = \sum_s P_{}(g_{} g_{}^\top + \mathrm{sum\_Pz}_{}\,\mathrm{sum\_Pz}_{}^\top), replacing SS separate rank-1 outer products one cache-efficient kernel. Identity D (optional): Per-draw dsyrk Z̃cs\tilde{Z}_c^s batch sum_Pzz_cc_s — available deferred; per-alt rank-1 loop retained. API change. Signature, return type, sign convention, WESML weight handling unchanged. Worst-case numerical deviation vs. oracle: 4.588×10−134.588 \times 10^{-13} (floating-point summation order ), 2,000 times inside 10−610^{-6} gate. 1,371 existing tests pass; CRAN: 0 errors / 0 warnings. title: The math behind choicer: Bayesian multinomial probit
text: Fully identified sampler (McCulloch, Polson & Rossi 2000): restrict σ11=1\sigma_{11} = 1 inside sampler, placing prior directly identified parameters. requires non-standard prior decomposition Σ\Sigma non-conjugate conditional, resulting chain known mix slowly. Non-identified chain post-processing (McCulloch & Rossi 1994; bayesm::rmnpGibbs approach): run Gibbs sampler unrestricted (β,Σ)(\beta, \Sigma) conjugate inverse-Wishart prior, report identified quantities β̃(r)=β(r)σ11(r),Σ̃(r)=Σ(r)σ11(r), \tilde{\beta}^{(r)} = \frac{\beta^{(r)}}{\sqrt{\sigma_{11}^{(r)}}}, \qquad \tilde{\Sigma}^{(r)} = \frac{\Sigma^{(r)}}{\sigma_{11}^{(r)}}, computed per draw rr.

title: The math behind choicer: Bayesian multinomial probit

Rationale: every Gibbs conditional remains standard (truncated normal, multivariate normal, inverse-Wishart), chain navigates posterior freely, reported quantities β̃,Σ̃\tilde{\beta}, \tilde{\Sigma} fully identified. caveat, stated McCulloch, Polson & Rossi (2000), prior identified parameters induced prior non-identified ones rather specified directly; diffuse defaults innocuous practice. title: The math behind choicer: Bayesian multinomial probit
text: Stacking latent regressions applying standard conjugate GLS algebra: β∣w,Σ∼N(β̃,Q−1),Q=+∑=1NXi′ΩXi,β̃=Q−1(Aβ‾+∑=1NXi′Ωwi). \beta \mid w, \Sigma \;\sim\; N\!\left(\tilde{\beta},\, Q^{-1}\right), \qquad Q = + \sum_{=1}^{N} X_i' \Omega X_i, \qquad \tilde{\beta} = Q^{-1}\!\left(\bar{\beta} + \sum_{=1}^{N} X_i' \Omega\, w_i\right). Two computational identities keep cheap: Precomputed Gram blocks. Let X(j)X_{(j)} N×KN \times K matrix collecting component-jj rows XiX_i, Gjk=X(j)′X(k)G_{jk} = X_{(j)}' X_{(k)}. ∑iXi′ΩXi=∑j=1p∑k=1pΩjkGjk. \sum_{} X_i' \Omega X_i = \sum_{j=1}^{p}\sum_{k=1}^{p} \Omega_{jk}\, G_{jk}. GjkG_{jk} depend chain computed startup; assembling QQ costs O(p2K2)O(p^2 K^2) per iteration, independent NN. inverse. Cholesky factorization Q=U′UQ = U'U (UU upper triangular), mean solves two triangular systems draw β=β̃+U−1z,z∼N(0,IK), \beta = \tilde{\beta} + U^{-1} z, \qquad z \sim N(0, I_K), Var⁡(U−1z)=Q−1\operatorname{Var}(U^{-1}z) = Q^{-1} without ever forming Q−1Q^{-1}. Code reference: mnprobit.cpp:153-176 (Gram blocks), mnprobit.cpp:254-278 (draw)

text: draw TN(μ,σ2;,b)\mathrm{TN}(\mu, \sigma^2; , b), standardize bounds (α,βu)=((−μ)/σ,(b−μ)/σ)(\alpha, \beta_u) = ((-\mu)/\sigma, (b-\mu)/\sigma) mirror interval upper half-line βu<0\beta_u < 0 (symmetry normal). Two regimes: Central case (α<4\alpha < 4): inverse CDF upper-tail scale, z=Φ‾−1(u),u∼Uniform(Φ‾(βu),Φ‾(α)), z = \bar{\Phi}^{-1}\!\big(u\big), \qquad u \sim \mathrm{Uniform}\big(\bar{\Phi}(\beta_u),\, \bar{\Phi}(\alpha)\big), Φ‾(x)=1−Φ(x)\bar{\Phi}(x) = 1 - \Phi(x). Working upper-tail probabilities keeps full floating-point precision bounds far tail, Φ(x)\Phi(x) rounds 1. (naive Φ−1(Uniform(Φ(),Φ(b)))\Phi^{-1}(\mathrm{Uniform}(\Phi(), \Phi(b))) returns ∞\infty ≳8a \gtrsim 8.) Tail case (α≥4\alpha \geq 4): Robert (1995) exponential rejection. rate λ=α+α2+42, \lambda = \frac{\alpha + \sqrt{\alpha^2 + 4}}{2}, propose z=α+E/λz = \alpha + E/\lambda E∼Exp(1)E \sim \mathrm{Exp}(1), reject z>βuz > \beta_u, accept probability exp⁡(−(z−λ)2/2)\exp\!\left(-(z - \lambda)^2 / 2\right). exact acceptance probability approaching 1 α→∞\alpha \\infty. title: Discrete choice from data to policy, in a dozen lines
text: mode_choice classic Greene & Hensher intercity travel-mode data: 210 travellers, choosing among air, train, bus car. ships package choicer’s long layout — one row per traveller alternative. wait (terminal waiting time) travel (-vehicle time) minutes; vcost travel cost. vary across modes within traveller, exactly choice model needs.
code: data(mode_choice) head(mode_choice, 4) #>   id  mode choice wait travel vcost gcost income size #> 1  1   air      0   69    100    59    70     35    1 #> 2  1 train      0   34    372    31    71     35    1 #> 3  1   bus      0   35    417    25    70     35    1 #> 4  1   car      1    0    180    10    30     35    1

title: Discrete choice from data to policy, in a dozen lines
text: Point identifier, alternative choice columns, list covariates, fit. Alternative-specific constants included default. Estimation takes fraction second. Travellers dislike waiting time, -vehicle time cost — three coefficients negative — summary() footer reports McFadden’s R² -sample hit rate alongside usual fit statistics.
code: fit <- run_mnlogit(   data           = mode_choice,   id_col         = "id",   alt_col        = "mode",   choice_col     = "choice",   covariate_cols = c("wait", "travel", "vcost") ) #> Optimization run time 0h:0m:0.01s summary(fit) #> Multinomial Logit (MNL) model #>  #> Parameter    Estimate  Std.Error  z-value  Pr(>|z|)   #> wait        -0.096887   0.010342  -9.3683  0.00e+00  *** #> travel      -0.003995   0.000849  -4.7043  2.55e-06  *** #> vcost       -0.013912   0.006651  -2.0916  3.65e-02  * #> ASC_train   -0.786669   0.602607  -1.3054  1.92e-01   #> ASC_bus     -1.433640   0.680713  -2.1061  3.52e-02  * #> ASC_car     -4.739865   0.867532  -5.4636  4.67e-08  *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 #>  #> Log-likelihood: -192.889  #> AIC: 397.777  | BIC: 417.86  #> McFadden R2: 0.337 (adj: 0.317) | Hit rate: 0.738  #> N: 210  | Parameters: 6  #> Optimization time: 0.01 s #> Convergence: 3 ( NLOPT_FTOL_REACHED: Optimization stopped because ftol_rel or ftol_abs (above) was reached. )

title: Discrete choice from data to policy, in a dozen lines
code: shares <- drop(predict(fit, type = "shares")) names(shares) <- as.character(fit$alt_mapping[[2]]) round(shares, 3) #>   air train   bus   car  #> 0.276 0.300 0.143 0.281  barplot(shares, col = "steelblue", ylab = "Predicted share",         main = "Predicted mode shares")

title: Discrete choice from data to policy, in a dozen lines
text: demand respond cost, go? fitted object answers , extra bookkeeping. -cost elasticities sit diagonal; -diagonal entries cross-elasticities. plain logit, substitution follows independence--irrelevant-alternatives (IIA) property — diversion share-proportional. (Relaxing IIA exactly nested logit mixed logit vignettes .)

title: Discrete choice from data to policy, in a dozen lines
text: vcost playing role price, wtp() returns marginal value attribute money units, delta-method standard errors. travellers’ implied value time: much pay shave minute -vehicle terminal time.
code: wtp(fit, price_var = "vcost") #> Willingness to pay (WTP), price variable: 'vcost' (95% CI) #>        Estimate Std_Error z_value CI_lower  CI_upper #> wait    -6.9645    3.4085  -2.043 -13.6450 -0.283902 #> travel  -0.2871    0.1436  -2.000  -0.5685 -0.005757

title: Discrete choice from data to policy, in a dozen lines
text: Suppose subsidy cuts cost train travel 25%. Perturb data predict — refitting required. Train’s share rises, drawing travellers away modes. can put money value change welfare expected consumer surplus: cheaper train fare raises expected consumer surplus, .
code: mc_cf <- mode_choice mc_cf$vcost[mc_cf$mode == "train"] <- mc_cf$vcost[mc_cf$mode == "train"] * 0.75  shares_cf <- drop(predict(fit, type = "shares", newdata = mc_cf)) names(shares_cf) <- names(shares)  rbind(baseline = shares, counterfactual = shares_cf) |> round(3) #>                  air train   bus   car #> baseline       0.276 0.300 0.143 0.281 #> counterfactual 0.269 0.324 0.138 0.270 cs0 <- consumer_surplus(fit, price_var = "vcost") cs1 <- consumer_surplus(fit, price_var = "vcost", newdata = mc_cf)  cs1$mean_cs - cs0$mean_cs # change in mean consumer surplus #> [1] 3.2

title: Discrete choice from data to policy, in a dozen lines
text: Everything — predict(), elasticities(), diversion_ratios(), wtp(), consumer_surplus(), plus blp() share inversion — works identically mixed logit (run_mxlogit()) nested logit (run_nestlogit()) fits, choicer also offers Bayesian multinomial probit sampler (run_mnprobit()). Learn model vignette: Multinomial logit — workhorse, parameter-recovery check Mixed logit — random coefficients preference heterogeneity Nested logit — correlated alternatives relaxed IIA Bayesian multinomial probit — flexible error covariance via MCMC

title: Discrete choice from data to policy, in a dozen lines
text: Several excellent R packages estimate discrete-choice models — among mlogit, logitr, gmnl, apollo mixl. choicer’s focus fast C++ core analytical gradients Hessians, single, consistent post-estimation toolkit aimed demand welfare quantities applied economists report.

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: utility individual ii derives alternative jj simulation draw ss : Vijs=Xijβ+Wij(μ*+γis*)+δj V_{ij}^s = X_{ij}\beta + W_{ij}\left(\mu^* + \gamma_i^{s*}\right) + \delta_j : - XijβX_{ij}\beta captures systematic utility fixed coefficients - Wijμ*W_{ij}\mu^* captures mean contribution random coefficients - Wijγis*W_{ij}\gamma_i^{s*} captures individual-specific random deviation - δj\delta_j alternative-specific constant (one normalized zero identification)

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: Random coefficients generated Cholesky decomposition structure: γis=Lηis,ηis∼N(0,IKw) \gamma_i^s = L \eta_i^s, \quad \text{} \eta_i^s \sim N(0, I_{K_w}) implies: γis∼N(0,Σ),Σ=LL′ \gamma_i^s \sim N(0, \Sigma), \quad \text{} \Sigma = LL' Cholesky parameterization ensures Σ\Sigma always positive semi-definite.

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: implementation supports two distributions random coefficient kk: Normal Distribution (rc_dist[k] = 0): μk*=μk,γiks*=γiks \mu_k^* = \mu_k, \quad \gamma_{ik}^{s*} = \gamma_{ik}^s Log-Normal Distribution (rc_dist[k] = 1): μk*=exp⁡(μk),γiks*=exp⁡(γiks) \mu_k^* = \exp(\mu_k), \quad \gamma_{ik}^{s*} = \exp(\gamma_{ik}^s) Note: shifted log-normal parameterization: βk=exp⁡(μk)+exp⁡(Lkη)\beta_k = \exp(\mu_k) + \exp(L_k \eta), differs textbook parameterization βk=exp⁡(μk+Lkη)\beta_k = \exp(\mu_k + L_k \eta).

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: full covariance matrix (rc_correlation = TRUE), lower-triangular matrix LL parameterized : L=(exp⁡(ℓ11)00⋯ℓ21exp⁡(ℓ22)0⋯ℓ31ℓ32exp⁡(ℓ33)⋯⋮⋮⋮⋱) L = \begin{pmatrix} \exp(\ell_{11}) & 0 & 0 & \cdots \\ \ell_{21} & \exp(\ell_{22}) & 0 & \cdots \\ \ell_{31} & \ell_{32} & \exp(\ell_{33}) & \cdots \\ \vdots & \vdots & \vdots & \ddots \end{pmatrix} : - Diagonal elements: Lpp=exp⁡(ℓpp)L_{pp} = \exp(\ell_{pp}) (ensures positivity) - -diagonal elements: Lpq=ℓpqL_{pq} = \ell_{pq} p>qp > q (unconstrained) diagonal-covariance (rc_correlation = FALSE): L=diag(exp(ℓ11),exp(ℓ22),…,exp(ℓKwKw)) L = \text{diag}\left(\exp(\ell_{11}), \exp(\ell_{22}), \ldots, \exp(\ell_{K_w K_w})\right) parameter vector LL length: - Full: Kw(Kw+1)/2K_w(K_w + 1)/2 - Diagonal: KwK_w

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: Motivation. columns XX WW span different magnitudes — e.g., price variable thousands alongside binary dummy — Hessian becomes ill-conditioned, L-BFGS step sizes mismatch across parameter blocks, exp⁡(Xβ)\exp(X\beta) can overflow log-sum-exp normalization kicks . Dividing column sample standard deviation removes data-unit dependence Hessian condition number can substantially accelerate convergence. centering omitted. Subtracting column mean XX shifts every utility global constant −βk⋅X⋅k¯-\beta_k \cdot \overline{X_{\cdot k}}, cancels soft-max within choice situation; analogous argument carries WW every draw η\eta. likelihood, gradient, Hessian therefore invariant global column centering, Z-transformation reduces scale--SD far optimizer concerned. skip centering practical grounds: destroys 0/1 structure dummy columns stored design matrices (complicating inspection downstream tooling) adds bookkeeping column means estimation benefit. transformation. Let skX=sd̂(X⋅k)s_k^X = \widehat{\text{sd}}(X_{\cdot k}) skW=sd̂(W⋅k)s_k^W = \widehat{\text{sd}}(W_{\cdot k}). Define scaled matrices X̃ijk=Xijk/skX,W̃ijk=Wijk/skW.\tilde X_{ijk} = X_{ijk} / s_k^X, \qquad \tilde W_{ijk} = W_{ijk} / s_k^W. Estimation runs (X̃,W̃)(\tilde X, \tilde W). Let β̃,μ̃,ℓ̃\tilde\beta, \tilde\mu, \tilde\ell denote parameter estimates scaled space. Back-transformation. optimization, parameters mapped back natural units: Cholesky rows: scaling column pp W̃\tilde W 1/spW1/s_p^W means row pp LL must scaled spWs_p^W keep utilities W̃ijk⋅(Lη)k\tilde W_{ijk} \cdot (L\eta)_k invariant. diagonal — LppL_{pp} stored ℓpp=log⁡Lpp\ell_{pp} = \log L_{pp} — multiplicative scaling becomes additive shift (ℓpp=ℓ̃pp−log⁡spW\ell_{pp} = \tilde\ell_{pp} - \log s_p^W); -diagonal entries direct multiplicative rescaling (ℓpq=ℓ̃pq/spW\ell_{pq} = \tilde\ell_{pq} / s_p^W). Note scale factor keyed row index pp cases — .e., random-coefficient associated chosen-row WW column — column index qq. Variance-covariance. back-transform linear stored parameters, delta method gives Vnatural=J⋅Vscaled⋅J⊤,V_{\text{natural}} = J \cdot V_{\text{scaled}} \cdot J^\top, JJ diagonal Jacobian natural--scaled map: entries 1/skX1/s_k^X βk\beta_k, 1/skW1/s_k^W μk\mu_k, 11 ℓpp\ell_{pp} (additive shift carries scale factor variance), 1/spW1/s_p^W ℓpq\ell_{pq}, 11 ASCs. Log-normal random coefficients. shifted log-normal parameterization βk=exp⁡(μk)+exp⁡((Lη)k)\beta_k = \exp(\mu_k) + \exp((L\eta)_k) admit closed-form back-transform multiplicative column scaling: dividing full distribution βk\beta_k skWs_k^W re-expressed single shifted log-normal shifted (μk,Lk,⋅)(\mu_k, L_{k,\cdot}), two exp\exp terms share common scale factor. implementation therefore passes log-normal WW columns unscaled: skW:=1s_k^W := 1 whenever rc_dist[k] == 1. user retains responsibility choosing reasonable units log-normal RC variables. Alternative scale denominators. scale_vars option also accepts "mad" (stats::mad, equal 1.4826×median|X⋅k−median(X⋅k)|1.4826 \times \text{median}|X_{\cdot k} - \text{median}(X_{\cdot k})|) "iqr" (stats::IQR(x) / 1.349); SD-equivalent normality yield Jacobian construction "sd" — per-column denominator skXs_k^X (skWs_k^W) differs. robust denominators preferable heavy-tailed columns cause sample SD dominated outliers.

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: given draw ss, probability individual ii chooses alternative jj follows logit formula: Pijs=exp⁡(Vijs)∑k=1Jiexp⁡(Viks) P_{ij}^s = \frac{\exp(V_{ij}^s)}{\sum_{k=1}^{J_i} \exp(V_{ik}^s)} outside option included, normalized Vi0s=0V_{i0}^s = 0.

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: Since random coefficients γi\gamma_i unobserved, integrate distribution using simulation: P‾iji=1S∑s=1SPijis \bar{P}_{ij_i} = \frac{1}{S} \sum_{s=1}^{S} P_{ij_i}^s jij_i alternative chosen individual ii.

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: simulated log-likelihood : ℓ(θ)=∑=1Nwilog⁡(P‾iji)=∑=1Nwilog⁡(1S∑s=1SPijis) \ell(\theta) = \sum_{=1}^{N} w_i \log\left(\bar{P}_{ij_i}\right) = \sum_{=1}^{N} w_i \log\left(\frac{1}{S} \sum_{s=1}^{S} P_{ij_i}^s\right) θ=(β,μ,vec(L),δ)\theta = (\beta, \mu, \text{vec}(L), \delta) full parameter vector.

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: avoid numerical overflow/underflow, implementation uses: Probability computation: computing PijsP_{ij}^s, subtract max⁡kViks\max_k V_{ik}^s: Pijs=exp⁡(Vijs−maxkViks)∑kexp⁡(Viks−maxkViks) P_{ij}^s = \frac{\exp(V_{ij}^s - \max_k V_{ik}^s)}{\sum_k \exp(V_{ik}^s - \max_k V_{ik}^s)} Log-probability averaging: Use log-sum-exp compute log⁡(∑sPijis)\log(\sum_s P_{ij_i}^s): logSumExp(,b)=max⁡(,b)+log⁡(exp(−max(,b))+exp(b−max(,b))) \text{logSumExp}(, b) = \max(,b) + \log\left(\exp(- \max(,b)) + \exp(b - \max(,b))\right) Applied iteratively across draws ss.

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: Define draw ss log-probability chosen alternative: log⁡Pijis=Vijis−log⁡(∑kexp(Viks)) \log P_{ij_i}^s = V_{ij_i}^s - \log\left(\sum_k \exp(V_{ik}^s)\right) gradient respect utility : ∂log⁡Pijis∂Vias=𝟏a=ji−Pias \frac{\partial \log P_{ij_i}^s}{\partial V_{ia}^s} = \mathbf{1}_{= j_i} - P_{ia}^s

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: Define zijs=∂Vijs∂θz_{ij}^s = \frac{\partial V_{ij}^s}{\partial \theta} gradient utility respect parameters. per-draw gradient : gis=∂log⁡Pijis∂θ=∑j=1Ji(𝟏j=ji−Pijs)zijs g_i^s = \frac{\partial \log P_{ij_i}^s}{\partial \theta} = \sum_{j=1}^{J_i} \left(\mathbf{1}_{j = j_i} - P_{ij}^s\right) z_{ij}^s Vectorized implementation. Define logit residual vector 𝐝is∈ℝJi\mathbf{d}_i^s \\mathbb{R}^{J_i} elements dijs=𝟏j=ji−Pijsd_{ij}^s = \mathbf{1}_{j = j_i} - P_{ij}^s. per-block gradient components collapse matrix-vector products — computed per draw rather looped alternatives: w̃=WiT𝐝is∈ℝKw\tilde{w}_i^s = W_i^T \mathbf{d}_i^s \\mathbb{R}^{K_w} (Wt_diff code) computed shared across μ\mu LL blocks. δ\delta block uses irregular alt-index scatter kept loop. Code reference: mxlogit.cpp:325-398

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: Using identity derivative logarithm sum: ∂∂θlog⁡(1S∑sPijis)=∑sPijis⋅gis∑sPijis \frac{\partial}{\partial \theta} \log\left(\frac{1}{S}\sum_s P_{ij_i}^s\right) = \frac{\sum_s P_{ij_i}^s \cdot g_i^s}{\sum_s P_{ij_i}^s} full gradient : ∇θℓ=∑=1Nwi⋅∑sPijis⋅gis∑sPijis \nabla_\theta \ell = \sum_{=1}^{N} w_i \cdot \frac{\sum_s P_{ij_i}^s \cdot g_i^s}{\sum_s P_{ij_i}^s} Code reference: mxlogit.cpp:400-409

Summing alternatives: gμ=w̃⊙dmu_final_dmug_\mu = \tilde{w}_i^s \odot \text{dmu\_final\_dmu}. Code reference: mxlogit.cpp:355-359

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: Let ℓpq\ell_{pq} denote parameter LpqL_{pq} (p≥qp \geq q). Step 1: Derivative LpqL_{pq} respect ℓpq\ell_{pq}: ∂Lpq∂ℓpq={Lpp=exp⁡(ℓpp)p=q (diagonal)1if p>q (-diagonal) \frac{\partial L_{pq}}{\partial \ell_{pq}} = \begin{cases} L_{pp} = \exp(\ell_{pp}) & \text{} p = q \text{ (diagonal)} \\ 1 & \text{} p > q \text{ (-diagonal)} \end{cases} Step 2: Derivative γps\gamma_p^s respect ℓpq\ell_{pq}: ∂γps∂ℓpq=∂Lpq∂ℓpq⋅ηqs \frac{\partial \gamma_p^s}{\partial \ell_{pq}} = \frac{\partial L_{pq}}{\partial \ell_{pq}} \cdot \eta_q^s Note: γps=∑r≤pLprηrs\gamma_p^s = \sum_{r \leq p} L_{pr} \eta_r^s, term r=qr = q contributes. Step 3: Derivative transformed γps*\gamma_p^{s*}: Normal distribution: ∂γps*∂γps=1 \frac{\partial \gamma_p^{s*}}{\partial \gamma_p^s} = 1 Log-Normal distribution: ∂γps*∂γps=exp⁡(γps)=γps* \frac{\partial \gamma_p^{s*}}{\partial \gamma_p^s} = \exp(\gamma_p^s) = \gamma_p^{s*} Step 4: Chain rule utility derivative: ∂Vijs∂ℓpq=Wijp⋅∂γps*∂γps⋅∂γps∂ℓpq \frac{\partial V_{ij}^s}{\partial \ell_{pq}} = W_{ijp} \cdot \frac{\partial \gamma_p^{s*}}{\partial \gamma_p^s} \cdot \frac{\partial \gamma_p^s}{\partial \ell_{pq}} Step 5: Summing alternatives using w̃=WiT𝐝is\tilde{w}_i^s = W_i^T \mathbf{d}_i^s: gℓpqs=(w̃)p⋅∂γps*∂γps⋅∂Lpq∂ℓpq⋅ηqs g_{\ell_{pq}}^s = (\tilde{w}_i^s)_p \cdot \frac{\partial \gamma_p^{s*}}{\partial \gamma_p^s} \cdot \frac{\partial L_{pq}}{\partial \ell_{pq}} \cdot \eta_q^s diagonal case, KwK_w parameters computed simultaneously Wt_diff % dgamma_final_dgamma % L.diag() % eta_i_s. full correlated case, Wt_p = Wt_diff(p) * dgamma_final_dgamma(p) factored per row pp. Code reference: mxlogit.cpp:361-381

Code reference: mxlogit.cpp:383-398

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: Define: - gi=∂log⁡P‾∂θg_i = \frac{\partial \log \bar{P}_i}{\partial \theta} — gradient individual ii - gis=∂log⁡Pijis∂θg_i^s = \frac{\partial \log P_{ij_i}^s}{\partial \theta} — per-draw gradient - =∂2log⁡Pijis∂θ∂θ′H_i^s = \frac{\partial^2 \log P_{ij_i}^s}{\partial \theta \partial \theta'} — per-draw Hessian Hessian simulated log-probability individual ii : Hi=∂2log⁡P‾∂θ∂θ′=∑sPijis(gis(gis)′+)∑sPijis−gigi′ H_i = \frac{\partial^2 \log \bar{P}_i}{\partial \theta \partial \theta'} = \frac{\sum_s P_{ij_i}^s \left(g_i^s (g_i^s)' + H_i^s\right)}{\sum_s P_{ij_i}^s} - g_i g_i' full Hessian : ∇θ2ℓ=∑=1Nwi⋅Hi \nabla^2_\theta \ell = \sum_{=1}^{N} w_i \cdot H_i Code reference: mxlogit.cpp:809-815

title: The math behind choicer: mixed logit
text: per-draw Hessian HisH_i^s form: =−∑jPijszijs(zijs)′+(∑jPijszijs)(∑jPijszijs)′+∑j(𝟏j=ji−Pijs)HV,ijs H_i^s = -\sum_j P_{ij}^s z_{ij}^s (z_{ij}^s)' + \left(\sum_j P_{ij}^s z_{ij}^s\right)\left(\sum_j P_{ij}^s z_{ij}^s\right)' + \sum_j \left(\mathbf{1}_{j=j_i} - P_{ij}^s\right) H_{V,ij}^s HV,ijs=∂2Vijs∂θ∂θ′H_{V,ij}^s = \frac{\partial^2 V_{ij}^s}{\partial \theta \partial \theta'} Hessian utility. code, computed : - sum_Pzz =∑jPijszijs(zijs)′= \sum_j P_{ij}^s z_{ij}^s (z_{ij}^s)' - sum_Pz =∑jPijszijs= \sum_j P_{ij}^s z_{ij}^s - sum_diff_H_V =∑j(𝟏j=ji−Pijs)HV,ijs= \sum_j (\mathbf{1}_{j=j_i} - P_{ij}^s) H_{V,ij}^s : =−sum_Pzz+sum_Pz⋅sum_Pz′+sum_diff_H_VH_i^s = -\text{sum\_Pzz} + \text{sum\_Pz} \cdot \text{sum\_Pz}' + \text{sum\_diff\_H\_V} Code reference: mxlogit.cpp:802

text: Since Lpp=exp⁡(ℓpp)L_{pp} = \exp(\ell_{pp}): ∂2Lpp∂ℓpp2=exp⁡(ℓpp)=Lpp \frac{\partial^2 L_{pp}}{\partial \ell_{pp}^2} = \exp(\ell_{pp}) = L_{pp} normal distribution: ∂2Vijs∂ℓpp2=Wijp⋅Lpp⋅ηps \frac{\partial^2 V_{ij}^s}{\partial \ell_{pp}^2} = W_{ijp} \cdot L_{pp} \cdot \eta_p^s log-normal distribution (combining chain rule): ∂2Vijs∂ℓpp2=Wijp[γps*(∂γps∂ℓpp)2+∂γps*∂γps⋅∂2γps∂ℓpp2] \frac{\partial^2 V_{ij}^s}{\partial \ell_{pp}^2} = W_{ijp} \left[ \gamma_p^{s*} \left(\frac{\partial \gamma_p^s}{\partial \ell_{pp}}\right)^2 + \frac{\partial \gamma_p^{s*}}{\partial \gamma_p^s} \cdot \frac{\partial^2 \gamma_p^s}{\partial \ell_{pp}^2} \right] ∂2γps∂ℓpp2=Lpp⋅ηps\frac{\partial^2 \gamma_p^s}{\partial \ell_{pp}^2} = L_{pp} \cdot \eta_p^s. text: random attribute coefficient βk(η)\beta_k(\eta) WTP random variable −βk(η)/α-\beta_k(\eta)/\alpha, scalar summary must chosen. summaries reported follow package’s parameterization (§1.3): Normal RC estimated mean (rc_mean = TRUE, rc_dist[k] = 0): βk=μk+(Lη)k\beta_k = \mu_k + (L\eta)_k mean μk\mu_k, mean WTP WTPk=−μkα \mathrm{WTP}_k = -\frac{\mu_k}{\alpha} linear-ratio gradient §10.1 applied (μk,α)(\mu_k, \alpha). Shifted log-normal RC estimated location (rc_mean = TRUE, rc_dist[k] = 1): βk=exp⁡(μk)+exp⁡((Lη)k)\beta_k = \exp(\mu_k) + \exp((L\eta)_k). Since βk\beta_k monotone normal draw (Lη)k(L\eta)_k median exp⁡((Lη)k)\exp((L\eta)_k) exp⁡(0)=1\exp(0) = 1, population median exp⁡(μk)+1\exp(\mu_k) + 1 median WTP WTPkmed=−exp⁡(μk)+1α,∂g∂μk=−exp⁡(μk)α,∂g∂α=exp⁡(μk)+1α2 \mathrm{WTP}_k^{\text{med}} = -\frac{\exp(\mu_k) + 1}{\alpha}, \qquad \frac{\partial g}{\partial \mu_k} = -\frac{\exp(\mu_k)}{\alpha}, \qquad \frac{\partial g}{\partial \alpha} = \frac{\exp(\mu_k) + 1}{\alpha^2} median preferred mean, exp⁡(μk)+exp⁡(σk2/2)\exp(\mu_k) + \exp(\sigma_k^2/2) σk2=(LLT)kk\sigma_k^2 = (LL^T)_{kk}, mean highly sensitive estimated variance (thin right tail dominates ). Shifted log-normal RC without location (rc_mean = FALSE, rc_dist[k] = 1): βk=exp⁡((Lη)k)\beta_k = \exp((L\eta)_k) median exactly 1, median WTP −1/α-1/\alpha ∂g/∂α=1/α2\partial g / \partial \alpha = 1/\alpha^2 — uncertainty comes solely α̂\hat\alpha. Normal RC without mean (rc_mean = FALSE, rc_dist[k] = 0): mean 0 construction; WTP row reported.

text: Conditional coefficient realization β(η)\beta(\eta), logsum MNL expression log⁡∑jexp⁡(Vij(η))\log \sum_j \exp(V_{ij}(\eta)). mixed logit logsum integrates mixing distribution: logsumi=𝔼η[log∑j∈Ciexp(Vij(η))]≈1S∑s=1Slog⁡∑j∈Ciexp⁡(Vijs) \mathrm{logsum}_i = \mathbb{E}_\eta\left[\log \sum_{j \C_i} \exp\big(V_{ij}(\eta)\big)\right] \;\approx\; \frac{1}{S} \sum_{s=1}^{S} \log \sum_{j \C_i} \exp\big(V_{ij}^s\big) simulated deterministic Halton draws used estimation (regenerated stored draw metadata), outside option contributing exp⁡(0)\exp(0) draw’s sum present, max-subtraction trick applied per draw. Order operations matters. log-sum-exp function convex, Jensen’s inequality 1S∑slse(Vs)≥lse(1S∑sVs), \frac{1}{S}\sum_s \mathrm{lse}(V^s) \;\geq\; \mathrm{lse}\left(\frac{1}{S}\sum_s V^s\right), strict inequality nondegenerate Σ\Sigma: taking logsum draw-averaged utilities (returned prediction kernel) systematically understates expected logsum. dedicated C++ kernel (mxl_logsum) therefore computes per-draw logsum averages across draws.

Summing alternatives, individual Hessian decomposes three blocks: −Hi/wi=(𝐁𝐁i𝐁𝐃i𝐁𝐃iT𝐃𝐃i)-H_i / w_i = \begin{pmatrix} \mathbf{BB}_i & \mathbf{BD}_i \\ \mathbf{BD}_i^T & \mathbf{DD}_i \end{pmatrix} Beta-beta block (K×KK \times K, symmetric): 𝐁𝐁i=∑=1mipiaxaxaT−μβμβT,μβ=∑=1mipiaxa\mathbf{BB}_i = \sum_{=1}^{m_i} p_{ia} \, x_a x_a^T - \mu_\beta \mu_\beta^T, \qquad \mu_\beta = \sum_{=1}^{m_i} p_{ia} \, x_a sum runs mim_i inside alternatives (since Z0=0Z_0 = 0). probability-weighted covariance covariates: 𝐁𝐁i=𝔼Pi[xxT]−𝔼Pi[x]𝔼Pi[x]T=Cov⁡Pi(x)\mathbf{BB}_i = \mathbb{E}_{P_i}[x x^T] - \mathbb{E}_{P_i}[x]\, \mathbb{E}_{P_i}[x]^T = \operatorname{Cov}_{P_i}(x) Delta-delta block (Jδ×JδJ_\delta \times J_\delta, symmetric): 𝐃𝐃i=diag⁡(μδ)−μδμδT,μδ[r]=∑{:d()=r}pia\mathbf{DD}_i = \operatorname{diag}(\mu_\delta) - \mu_\delta \mu_\delta^T, \qquad \mu_\delta[r] = \sum_{\{: d()=r\}} p_{ia} one alternative maps free ASC index, μδ\mu_\delta vector choice probabilities ASC-bearing alternatives. diagonal 𝐃𝐃i\mathbf{DD}_i μδ⊙(1−μδ)\mu_\delta \odot (1 - \mu_\delta) -diagonal (r,s)(r,s) entry −μδ[r]μδ[s]-\mu_\delta[r]\,\mu_\delta[s]. Beta-delta block (K×JδK \times J_\delta, generally symmetric): 𝐁𝐃i[:,r]=∑{:d()=r}piaxa−μβμδ[r]\mathbf{BD}_i[:,r] = \sum_{\{: d()=r\}} p_{ia} \, x_a - \mu_\beta \, \mu_\delta[r] block measures probability-weighted covariance covariates ASC indicators. decomposition equals full-vector form. Partition Za=(xaT,ed()T)TZ_a = (x_a^T, e_{d()}^T)^T. : ∑apiaZaZaT=(∑apiaxaxaT∑apiaxaed()T∑apiaed()xaT∑apiaed()ed()T)\sum_a p_{ia} Z_a Z_a^T = \begin{pmatrix} \sum_a p_{ia} x_a x_a^T & \sum_a p_{ia} x_a e_{d()}^T \\ \sum_a p_{ia} e_{d()} x_a^T & \sum_a p_{ia} e_{d()} e_{d()}^T \end{pmatrix} (∑apiaZa)(∑apiaZa)T=(μβμβTμβμδTμδμβTμδμδT)\left(\sum_a p_{ia} Z_a\right)\left(\sum_a p_{ia} Z_a\right)^T = \begin{pmatrix} \mu_\beta \mu_\beta^T & \mu_\beta \mu_\delta^T \\ \mu_\delta \mu_\beta^T & \mu_\delta \mu_\delta^T \end{pmatrix} Subtracting block block yields exactly 𝐁𝐁i\mathbf{BB}_i, 𝐁𝐃i\mathbf{BD}_i, 𝐃𝐃i\mathbf{DD}_i .

title: The math behind choicer: multinomial logit
text: 𝐁𝐁i\mathbf{BB}_i 𝐃𝐃i\mathbf{DD}_i symmetric. implementation accumulates upper triangles blocks inner loop alternatives reflects loop, halving write operations blocks: BB: double loop r 0..K-1, c r..K-1 accumulates piaxarxacp_{ia} x_{ar} x_{ac} upper triangle; lower triangle filled mirroring. DD: μδ\mu_\delta length-JδJ_\delta vector; matrix diag⁡(μδ)−μδμδT\operatorname{diag}(\mu_\delta) - \mu_\delta \mu_\delta^T assembled vector alternatives processed, upper triangle mirrored. BD: K×JδK \times J_\delta rectangular — full block computed (symmetry available across two index spaces). existing final symmetrization global_hess = 0.5*(global_hess + global_hess^T) remains numerical tidy-. Complexity. block assembly costs O(J⋅K2/2)O(J \cdot K^2 / 2) BB, O(J⋅K)O(J \cdot K) BD scatter, O(Jδ2/2)O(J_\delta^2 / 2) DD — versus O(J⋅(K+Jδ)2)O(J \cdot (K + J_\delta)^2) naive dense outer product. asymptotic gain scales (1+Jδ/K)2(1 + J_\delta/K)^2, material number free ASC parameters comparable larger KK.

title: The math behind choicer: multinomial logit
text: elasticity measures percentage change choice probability respect percentage change attribute. attribute kk coefficient βk\beta_k: -Elasticity (elasticity PijP_{ij} respect xijkx_{ijk}): Ejjk=∂Pij∂xijk⋅xijkPij=βk⋅xijk⋅(1−Pij) E_{jj}^k = \frac{\partial P_{ij}}{\partial x_{ijk}} \cdot \frac{x_{ijk}}{P_{ij}} = \beta_k \cdot x_{ijk} \cdot (1 - P_{ij}) Cross-Elasticity (elasticity PijP_{ij} respect ximkx_{imk} m≠jm \neq j): Ejmk=∂Pij∂ximk⋅ximkPij=−βk⋅ximk⋅Pim E_{jm}^k = \frac{\partial P_{ij}}{\partial x_{imk}} \cdot \frac{x_{imk}}{P_{ij}} = -\beta_k \cdot x_{imk} \cdot P_{im}

title: The math behind choicer: multinomial logit
text: Starting choice probability: Pij=exp⁡(Vij)∑kexp⁡(Vik) P_{ij} = \frac{\exp(V_{ij})}{\sum_k \exp(V_{ik})} Taking derivative respect ximkx_{imk}: ∂Pij∂ximk=Pij(𝟏j=m−Pim)βk \frac{\partial P_{ij}}{\partial x_{imk}} = P_{ij} \left( \mathbf{1}_{j=m} - P_{im} \right) \beta_k -elasticity (j=mj = m): ∂Pij∂xijk=Pij(1−Pij)βk \frac{\partial P_{ij}}{\partial x_{ijk}} = P_{ij} (1 - P_{ij}) \beta_k Therefore: Ejjk=βk⋅xijk⋅(1−Pij) E_{jj}^k = \beta_k \cdot x_{ijk} \cdot (1 - P_{ij}) cross-elasticity (j≠mj \neq m): ∂Pij∂ximk=−PijPimβk \frac{\partial P_{ij}}{\partial x_{imk}} = -P_{ij} P_{im} \beta_k Therefore: Ejmk=−βk⋅ximk⋅Pim E_{jm}^k = -\beta_k \cdot x_{imk} \cdot P_{im}

Every alternative individual ii’s choice set equally likely: ℓ0=−∑=1Nwilog⁡(Mi+𝟏outside) \ell_0 = -\sum_{=1}^{N} w_i \log\left(M_i + \mathbf{1}_{\text{outside}}\right) MiM_i number inside alternatives. closed form exact unbalanced choice sets arbitrary weights. Market shares. maximized log-likelihood constants-(ASC-) model. every alternative available every choice situation, ASC-model’s fitted probabilities equal observed market shares sjs_j, giving closed form ℓ0=∑jNjlog⁡(sj) \ell_0 = \sum_{j} N_j \log(s_j) NjN_j choice counts (outside option enters category present; never-chosen alternatives contribute 0⋅log⁡0=00 \cdot \log 0 = 0). closed form requires identical choice-set composition across individuals — equal set sizes sufficient — uniform weights; otherwise ASC-model must refit explicitly.

text: robust recipe mixed logit: warm-start plain MNL, scale variables Hessian well conditioned, use enough Halton draws. estimate full (correlated) covariance random coefficients. Diversion therefore depends attribute changing, diversion_ratios() takes wrt_var: rest toolkit — predict(), wtp(), consumer_surplus(), blp() — behaves exactly getting-started vignette, integrating distribution tastes automatically.

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: utility individual ii derives alternative jj : Uij=Vij+εij U_{ij} = V_{ij} + \varepsilon_{ij} systematic utility : Vij=Xijβ+δj V_{ij} = X_{ij}\beta + \delta_j error term εij\varepsilon_{ij} follows Generalized Extreme Value (GEV) distribution induces within-nest correlation.

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: JJ inside alternatives partitioned KK mutually exclusive exhaustive nests B1,…,BKB_1, \ldots, B_K. outside option (alternative 0) Vi0=0V_{i0} = 0 may optionally included, always assigned singleton nest λ0=1\lambda_0 = 1.

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: nest kk dissimilarity parameter λk>0\lambda_k > 0 governs within-nest substitution: λk=1\lambda_k = 1 corresponds independent (MNL-like) alternatives within nest. 0<λk<10 < \lambda_k < 1 indicates positive within-nest correlation (typical case). λk>1\lambda_k > 1 theoretically permissible implies negative correlation. Singleton nests (nests exactly one alternative) λk\lambda_k fixed 1 estimated. non-singleton nests contribute λ\lambda parameters θ\theta. Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:61-104

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: ASC identification follows convention MNL: Without outside option (include_outside_option = FALSE): first inside alternative’s ASC fixed zero (δ1=0\delta_1 = 0). parameter vector contains J−1J-1 free ASC parameters. outside option (include_outside_option = TRUE): outside option serves reference Vi0=0V_{i0} = 0. JJ inside alternatives free ASC parameters. Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:107-135

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: nested logit choice probability decomposes : Pij=P(j∣k(j))⋅Pk(j) P_{ij} = P(j \mid k(j)) \cdot P_{k(j)} two components derived GEV generating function.

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: inclusive value nest kk log-sum scaled utilities within nest: Ik=∑j∈Bkexp⁡(Vijλk) I_k = \sum_{j \B_k} \exp\!\left(\frac{V_{ij}}{\lambda_k}\right) log⁡Ik=log⁡(∑j∈Bkexp(Vijλk)) \log I_k = \log\!\left(\sum_{j \B_k} \exp\!\left(\frac{V_{ij}}{\lambda_k}\right)\right)

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: probability choosing alternative jj given nest k(j)k(j) chosen: P(j∣k)=exp⁡(Vij/λk)Ik P(j \mid k) = \frac{\exp(V_{ij}/\lambda_k)}{I_k} log form: log⁡P(j∣k)=Vijλk−log⁡Ik \log P(j \mid k) = \frac{V_{ij}}{\lambda_k} - \log I_k

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: probability choosing nest kk: Pk=exp⁡(λklog⁡Ik)∑lexp⁡(λllog⁡Il) P_k = \frac{\exp(\lambda_k \log I_k)}{\sum_{l} \exp(\lambda_l \log I_l)} outside option included, contribution enters denominator exp⁡(λ0log⁡I0)=exp⁡(1⋅log⁡(exp⁡(0/1)))=exp⁡(0)=1\exp(\lambda_0 \log I_0) = \exp(1 \cdot \log(\exp(0/1))) = \exp(0) = 1. log form: log⁡Pk=λklog⁡Ik−log⁡(∑lexp(λllogIl)) \log P_k = \lambda_k \log I_k - \log\!\left(\sum_l \exp(\lambda_l \log I_l)\right)

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: Taking logs joint probability: log⁡Pij=log⁡P(j∣k(j))+log⁡Pk(j) \log P_{ij} = \log P(j \mid k(j)) + \log P_{k(j)} log⁡Pij=Vijλk(j)−log⁡Ik(j)+λk(j)log⁡Ik(j)−log⁡Di \log P_{ij} = \frac{V_{ij}}{\lambda_{k(j)}} - \log I_{k(j)} + \lambda_{k(j)} \log I_{k(j)} - \log D_i Di=∑lexp⁡(λllog⁡Il)D_i = \sum_l \exp(\lambda_l \log I_l) (plus 1 outside option present). Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:235-260

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: log-likelihood weighted sum log-probabilities observed choices: ℓ(θ)=∑=1Nwilog⁡Piji \ell(\theta) = \sum_{=1}^{N} w_i \log P_{ij_i} θ=(β,λ,δ)\theta = (\beta, \lambda, \delta) jij_i alternative chosen individual ii. Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:263-266

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: Two log-sum-exp stabilizations applied. Within-nest inclusive value. compute log⁡Ik\log I_k without overflow, implementation subtracts maximum scaled utility nest: log⁡Ik=Vmax⁡,k+log⁡(∑j∈Bkexp(Vijλk−Vmax⁡,k)) \log I_k = V_{\max,k} + \log\!\left(\sum_{j \B_k} \exp\!\left(\frac{V_{ij}}{\lambda_k} - V_{\max,k}\right)\right) Vmax⁡,k=max⁡j∈BkVij/λkV_{\max,k} = \max_{j \B_k} V_{ij}/\lambda_k. Across-nest denominator. denominator Di=∑lexp⁡(λllog⁡Il)D_i = \sum_l \exp(\lambda_l \log I_l) similarly stabilized subtracting max⁡lλllog⁡Il\max_l \lambda_l \log I_l exponentiation. Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:190-230

what: 3.1 Gradient with Respect to ViaV_{ia} (Utility Scores)
title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: derive gradients respect β\beta δ\delta, first compute derivative log-probability chosen alternative respect systematic utility ViaV_{ia} alternative aa. Let kik_i denote chosen nest (.e., k(ji)k(j_i)). log-probability chosen alternative : log⁡Piji=log⁡P(ji∣ki)+log⁡Pki \log P_{ij_i} = \log P(j_i \mid k_i) + \log P_{k_i} Derivative conditional term. Since log⁡P(j∣k)=Vij/λk−log⁡Ik\log P(j \mid k) = V_{ij}/\lambda_k - \log I_k ∂log⁡Ik∂Via=1λk𝟏a∈BkP(∣k)\frac{\partial \log I_k}{\partial V_{ia}} = \frac{1}{\lambda_k} \mathbf{1}_{\B_k} P(\mid k): ∂log⁡P(ji∣ki)∂Via=1λki(𝟏a=ji−𝟏a∈BkiP(∣ki)) \frac{\partial \log P(j_i \mid k_i)}{\partial V_{ia}} = \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}}\left(\mathbf{1}_{= j_i} - \mathbf{1}_{\B_{k_i}} P(\mid k_i)\right) Derivative nest term. Using ∂log⁡Di∂Via=Pk()P(∣k())=Pia\frac{\partial \log D_i}{\partial V_{ia}} = P_{k()} P(\mid k()) = P_{ia}: ∂log⁡Pki∂Via=𝟏a∈BkiP(∣ki)−Pia \frac{\partial \log P_{k_i}}{\partial V_{ia}} = \mathbf{1}_{\B_{k_i}} P(\mid k_i) - P_{ia} Combined. Adding terms: ∂log⁡Piji∂Via=𝟏a=jiλki+(1−1λki)𝟏a∈BkiP(∣ki)−Pia \frac{\partial \log P_{ij_i}}{\partial V_{ia}} = \frac{\mathbf{1}_{= j_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}} + \left(1 - \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}}\right)\mathbf{1}_{\B_{k_i}} P(\mid k_i) - P_{ia} implemented vector grad_vec length JiJ_i: grad_vec[]=−Pia+(1−1λki)𝟏a∈BkiP(∣ki)+1λki𝟏a=ji \text{grad\_vec}[] = -P_{ia} + \left(1 - \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}}\right) \mathbf{1}_{\B_{k_i}} P(\mid k_i) + \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}} \mathbf{1}_{= j_i} Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:284-295

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: Since ∂Vij∂β=XijT\frac{\partial V_{ij}}{\partial \beta} = X_{ij}^T, contribution individual ii : ∂ℓi∂β=wi∑j=1Jigrad_vec[j]⋅XijT=wiXiTgrad_vec \frac{\partial \ell_i}{\partial \beta} = w_i \sum_{j=1}^{J_i} \text{grad\_vec}[j] \cdot X_{ij}^T = w_i \, X_i^T \, \text{grad\_vec} full gradient accumulated single BLAS matrix-vector product, identically MNL. Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:297-298

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: Since ∂Vij∂δa=𝟏j=\frac{\partial V_{ij}}{\partial \delta_a} = \mathbf{1}_{j = }, gradient entries corresponding elements grad_vec, scattered appropriate positions θ\theta: ∂ℓi∂δa=wi⋅grad_vec[] \frac{\partial \ell_i}{\partial \delta_a} = w_i \cdot \text{grad\_vec}[] free-ASC convention MNL applies (first inside alternative’s ASC excluded outside option). Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:300-312

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: Define within-nest probability-weighted mean utility: V‾k=∑j∈BkP(j∣k)⋅Vij \bar{V}_k = \sum_{j \B_k} P(j \mid k) \cdot V_{ij} chosen nest (k=kik = k_i). log-probability decomposes : log⁡Piji=Vijiλki−log⁡Iki⏟log⁡P(ji∣ki)+λkilog⁡Iki−log⁡Di⏟log⁡Pki \log P_{ij_i} = \underbrace{\frac{V_{ij_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}} - \log I_{k_i}}_{\log P(j_i \mid k_i)} + \underbrace{\lambda_{k_i} \log I_{k_i} - \log D_i}_{\log P_{k_i}} Using ∂log⁡Ik∂λk=−V‾k/λk2\frac{\partial \log I_k}{\partial \lambda_k} = -\bar{V}_k / \lambda_k^2: ∂log⁡P(ji∣ki)∂λki=V‾ki−Vijiλki2 \frac{\partial \log P(j_i \mid k_i)}{\partial \lambda_{k_i}} = \frac{\bar{V}_{k_i} - V_{ij_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}^2} ∂log⁡Pki∂λki=(1−Pki)(logIki−V‾kiλki) \frac{\partial \log P_{k_i}}{\partial \lambda_{k_i}} = (1 - P_{k_i})\!\left(\log I_{k_i} - \frac{\bar{V}_{k_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}}\right) Combining: ∂ℓi∂λki=wi[(1−Pki)(logIki−V‾kiλki)+V‾ki−Vijiλki2] \frac{\partial \ell_i}{\partial \lambda_{k_i}} = w_i \left[(1 - P_{k_i})\!\left(\log I_{k_i} - \frac{\bar{V}_{k_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}}\right) + \frac{\bar{V}_{k_i} - V_{ij_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}^2}\right] non-chosen nest (k≠kik \neq k_i). conditional probability P(ji∣ki)P(j_i \mid k_i) independent λk\lambda_k, nest probability contributes: ∂log⁡Pki∂λk=−Pk(logIk−V‾kλk) \frac{\partial \log P_{k_i}}{\partial \lambda_k} = -P_k \!\left(\log I_k - \frac{\bar{V}_k}{\lambda_k}\right) ∂ℓi∂λk=−wiPk(logIk−V‾kλk) \frac{\partial \ell_i}{\partial \lambda_k} = -w_i \, P_k \!\left(\log I_k - \frac{\bar{V}_k}{\lambda_k}\right) common factor log⁡Ik−V‾k/λk\log I_k - \bar{V}_k / \lambda_k labeled term_in_brackets code. Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:314-342

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: section documents Hessian negated log-likelihood. function nl_loglik_hessian_parallel returns H(θ)=−∇2ℓ(θ)H(\theta) = -\nabla^2 \ell(\theta), quantity invert_hessian() expects (positive semi-definite MLE). sign convention matches numeric oracle nl_loglik_numeric_hessian (differences negated gradient), functions interchangeable inputs invert_hessian(). Two implementations available: nl_loglik_hessian_parallel — default (se_method = "hessian"): single-pass analytical Hessian derived . Validated numeric oracle max absolute difference ≈2.6×10−8\approx 2.6 \times 10^{-8} across test fixtures (Fixtures –), well 10−610^{-6} equivalence gate. extreme λk<0.1\lambda_k < 0.1, oracle’s finite-difference accuracy degrades; analytical Hessian remains finite symmetric throughout. nl_loglik_numeric_hessian — retained oracle (se_method = "numeric"): central finite differences analytical gradient step ϵi=10−6⋅max⁡(|θi|,1)\epsilon_i = 10^{-6} \cdot \max(|\theta_i|, 1). Use diagnostic comparison whenever direct verification analytical path required.

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: notation follows §§0–3. Subscripts ii (individual), j//bj//b (alternative local indices within ii’s choice set, 0-based), kk/ll (nest indices, 0-based) used throughout. Additional symbols specific section: negated per-individual log-likelihood contribution fi=−wilog⁡Pi,caf_i = -w_i \log P_{,c_a}. full Hessian sum H=∑=1NH()H = \sum_{=1}^N H^{()} H()=−wi∇2log⁡Pi,caH^{()} = -w_i \nabla^2 \log P_{,c_a}.

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: Vij=𝐱ijβ+δjV_{ij} = \mathbf{x}_{ij} \beta + \delta_j linear β\beta δ\delta, second derivatives respect parameters factor mi×mim_i \times m_i V-space Hessian. Define: Qi[,b]=−∂2log⁡Pi,ca∂Via∂Vib Q_i[, b] = -\frac{\partial^2 \log P_{,c_a}}{\partial V_{ia} \, \partial V_{ib}} Starting gradient formula (§3.1): g[]=∂log⁡Pi,ca∂Via=−Pia+(1−1λki)𝟏a∈BkiP(∣ki)+𝟏a=caλki g[] = \frac{\partial \log P_{,c_a}}{\partial V_{ia}} = -P_{ia} + \left(1 - \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}}\right) \mathbf{1}_{\B_{k_i}} P(\mid k_i) + \frac{\mathbf{1}_{= c_a}}{\lambda_{k_i}} differentiating respect VibV_{ib} yields: ∂g[]∂Vib=−∂Pia∂Vib+(1−1λki)𝟏a∈Bki∂P(∣ki)∂Vib \frac{\partial g[]}{\partial V_{ib}} = -\frac{\partial P_{ia}}{\partial V_{ib}} + \left(1 - \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}}\right) \mathbf{1}_{\B_{k_i}} \frac{\partial P(\mid k_i)}{\partial V_{ib}} Partial derivative PiaP_{ia} respect VibV_{ib}. Write ka=k()k_a = k(), kb=k(b)k_b = k(b). Expanding Pia=P(∣ka)⋅PkaP_{ia} = P(\mid k_a) \cdot P_{k_a} via product rule using ∂Pka/∂Vib=Pka[𝟏kb=kaP(b∣ka)−Pib]\partial P_{k_a}/\partial V_{ib} = P_{k_a}[\mathbf{1}_{k_b=k_a} P(b\mid k_a) - P_{ib}]: ∂Pia∂Vib=Pia{𝟏ka=kb⋅P(b∣ka)[1+𝟏a=b−P(∣ka)λka]−Pib} \frac{\partial P_{ia}}{\partial V_{ib}} = P_{ia} \left\{   \mathbf{1}_{k_a = k_b} \cdot P(b \mid k_a) \left[1 + \frac{\mathbf{1}_{=b} - P(\mid k_a)}{\lambda_{k_a}}\right]   - P_{ib} \right\} Partial derivative P(∣ki)P(\mid k_i) respect VibV_{ib}. Non-zero ∈Bkia \B_{k_i}: ∂P(∣ki)∂Vib=1λki𝟏kb=kiP(b∣ki)(𝟏a=b−P(∣ki)) \frac{\partial P(\mid k_i)}{\partial V_{ib}} = \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}} \mathbf{1}_{k_b = k_i} P(b \mid k_i) \left(\mathbf{1}_{=b} - P(\mid k_i)\right) Combined V-space Hessian (correct formula). Negating combining: Qi[,b]=Pia{𝟏ka=kbP(b∣ka)[1+𝟏a=b−P(∣ka)λka]−Pib}−𝟏ka=ki𝟏kb=ki⋅1−1/λkiλki⋅P(b∣ki)(𝟏a=b−P(∣ki))⏟Ca,b: chosen-nest correction \boxed{ Q_i[, b] = P_{ia} \left\{   \mathbf{1}_{k_a = k_b} P(b \mid k_a) \left[1 + \frac{\mathbf{1}_{=b} - P(\mid k_a)}{\lambda_{k_a}}\right]   - P_{ib} \right\} - \underbrace{\mathbf{1}_{k_a = k_i} \mathbf{1}_{k_b = k_i} \cdot \frac{1 - 1/\lambda_{k_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}} \cdot P(b \mid k_i)\bigl(\mathbf{1}_{=b} - P(\mid k_i)\bigr)}_{C_{,b}: \text{ chosen-nest correction}} } chosen-nest correction Ca,bC_{,b} nonzero aa bb chosen nest kik_i (ki≥0k_i \geq 0). arises (1−1/λki)(1 - 1/\lambda_{k_i}) factor g[]g[] coupling conditional probability gradient back VibV_{ib}. Structural notes MNL limit: ka≠kbk_a \neq k_b: Qi[,b]=−PiaPibQ_i[,b] = -P_{ia} P_{ib} (cross-nest entries equal product marginal probabilities, familiar multinomial variance term). MNL limit (λk=1\lambda_k = 1 kk): Qi[,b]=Pia(𝟏a=b−Pib)Q_i[,b] = P_{ia}(\mathbf{1}_{=b} - P_{ib}), standard multinomial Hessian −log⁡Pca-\log P_{c_a}. diagonal =Pia(1−Pia)= P_{ia}(1-P_{ia}); -diagonal =−PiaPib= -P_{ia}P_{ib}. chosen-nest correction vanishes 1−1/λki=01 - 1/\lambda_{k_i} = 0. outside option chosen (ki=−1k_i = -1) λki=1\lambda_{k_i} = 1 (singleton chosen nest), Ca,b=0C_{,b} = 0 pairs. Implementation note: implementation materializes QiQ_i explicit mi×mim_i \times m_i matrix, efficient typical choice set sizes (J≤20J \leq 20). outer-product decomposition used beta-beta block (§4.3) avoids forming matrix explicitly K×KK \times K accumulation. Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:563–601

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: Since ∂Vij/∂βr=xij,r\partial V_{ij}/\partial \beta_r = x_{ij,r}: Hβr,βs()=wi∑∑bxia,r⋅Qi[,b]⋅xib,s=wi𝐗iT𝐐i𝐗i H^{()}_{\beta_r, \beta_s} = w_i \sum_{} \sum_{b} x_{ia,r} \cdot Q_i[,b] \cdot x_{ib,s} = w_i \, \mathbf{X}_i^T \mathbf{Q}_i \mathbf{X}_i matrix product decomposed three terms can accumulated single nest-level pass, exploiting outer-product structure QiQ_i: Term — universal outer product (subtracted): −wi𝐱‾i𝐱‾-w_i \, \bar{\mathbf{x}}_i \, \bar{\mathbf{x}}_i^T arises −PiaPib-P_{ia} P_{ib} term universal pairs: ∑,bxia,rPiaPibxib,s=x‾,rx‾,s\sum_{,b} x_{ia,r} P_{ia} P_{ib} x_{ib,s} = \bar{x}_{,r} \bar{x}_{,s}. Term B — per-nest within-nest correction (added), summed nests kk: +wi∑k[(1−1λk)𝐦k𝐜kT+1λk𝐒k(2)] +w_i \sum_{k} \left[ \left(1 - \frac{1}{\lambda_k}\right) \mathbf{m}_k \mathbf{c}_k^T + \frac{1}{\lambda_k} \mathbf{S}^{(2)}_k \right] singleton nests (λk=1\lambda_k = 1): (1−1/λk)=0(1 - 1/\lambda_k) = 0, contribution reduces 𝐒k(2)\mathbf{S}^{(2)}_k. Term C — chosen-nest correction (subtracted), ki≥0k_i \geq 0: −wi⋅1−1/λkiλki(𝐐ki−𝐜ki𝐜kiT) -w_i \cdot \frac{1 - 1/\lambda_{k_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}} \left( \mathbf{Q}_{k_i} - \mathbf{c}_{k_i} \mathbf{c}_{k_i}^T \right) 𝐐ki−𝐜ki𝐜kiT\mathbf{Q}_{k_i} - \mathbf{c}_{k_i}\mathbf{c}_{k_i}^T P(⋅∣ki)P(\cdot\mid k_i)-weighted covariance XX within chosen nest. Full beta-beta block (Hessian negated log-likelihood; positive semi-definite MLE): Hββ()=wi[−𝐱‾i𝐱‾+∑k((1−1λk)𝐦k𝐜kT+1λk𝐒k(2))−𝟏ki≥0⋅1−1/λkiλki(𝐐ki−𝐜ki𝐜kiT)] H^{()}_{\beta\beta} = w_i \left[   -\bar{\mathbf{x}}_i \bar{\mathbf{x}}_i^T   + \sum_k \!\left(\!\left(1-\frac{1}{\lambda_k}\right)\mathbf{m}_k \mathbf{c}_k^T + \frac{1}{\lambda_k}\mathbf{S}^{(2)}_k\right)   - \mathbf{1}_{k_i \geq 0} \cdot \frac{1-1/\lambda_{k_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}} \!\left(\mathbf{Q}_{k_i} - \mathbf{c}_{k_i}\mathbf{c}_{k_i}^T\right) \right] MNL limit check. λk=1\lambda_k = 1 kk: Term B =∑k𝐒k(2)=𝐗iTdiag(Pi)𝐗i= \sum_k \mathbf{S}^{(2)}_k = \mathbf{X}_i^T \mathrm{diag}(P_i) \mathbf{X}_i; Term C vanishes; formula yields standard MNL expression wi(𝐗iTdiag(Pi)𝐗i−𝐱‾i𝐱‾)w_i(\mathbf{X}_i^T \mathrm{diag}(P_i) \mathbf{X}_i - \bar{\mathbf{x}}_i \bar{\mathbf{x}}_i^T). Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:651–682

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: Since ∂Vij/∂δa=𝟏j=\partial V_{ij}/\partial \delta_a = \mathbf{1}_{j=}, blocks direct projections QiQ_i onto free-ASC alternatives. Delta-delta block (free-ASC alternatives ′' b′b'): Hδa′,δb′()=wi⋅Qi[′,b′] H^{()}_{\delta_{'}, \delta_{b'}} = w_i \cdot Q_i[', b'] Beta-delta block (covariate index rr free-ASC alternative b′b'): Hβr,δb′()=wi∑axia,r⋅Qi[,b′]=wi(𝐗iT𝐐i[:,b′])r H^{()}_{\beta_r, \delta_{b'}} = w_i \sum_{} x_{ia,r} \cdot Q_i[, b'] = w_i \, \bigl(\mathbf{X}_i^T \mathbf{Q}_i[:, b']\bigr)_r blocks reuse QiQ_i matrix computed §4.2. additional auxiliary quantities required. ASC normalization. Without outside option, first inside alternative’s ASC fixed zero: free-ASC alternatives satisfy alt_idx0[] > 0, mapped theta positions delta_start_idx + alt_idx0[] - 1. Code reference: nestlogit.cpp:604–648

title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: lambda parameters enter nonlinearly log⁡Ik\log I_k nest probabilities, cross-derivatives λk\lambda_k β/δ\beta/\delta must computed via chain rule. Define: db(k)=∂gλk∂Vib,gλk=∂log⁡Pi,ca∂λk d^{(k)}_b = \frac{\partial g_{\lambda_k}}{\partial V_{ib}}, \qquad g_{\lambda_k} = \frac{\partial \log P_{,c_a}}{\partial \lambda_k} Key intermediates (derived §3.4): ∂Tk∂Vib=−𝟏k(b)=kP(b∣k)Vib−V‾kλk2,∂Pk∂Vib=Pk[𝟏k(b)=kP(b∣k)−Pib] \frac{\partial T_k}{\partial V_{ib}} = -\mathbf{1}_{k(b)=k} P(b \mid k) \frac{V_{ib} - \bar{V}_k}{\lambda_k^2}, \qquad \frac{\partial P_k}{\partial V_{ib}} = P_k \bigl[\mathbf{1}_{k(b)=k} P(b \mid k) - P_{ib}\bigr] non-chosen nests (k≠kik \neq k_i), using gλk=−PkTkg_{\lambda_k} = -P_k T_k: db(k)=PkTkPib+𝟏k(b)=kPkP(b∣k)[Vib−V‾kλk2−Tk] d^{(k)}_b = P_k T_k P_{ib} + \mathbf{1}_{k(b)=k} P_k P(b \mid k) \left[\frac{V_{ib} - \bar{V}_k}{\lambda_k^2} - T_k\right] first term universal “shift” proportional PibP_{ib}; second within-nest correction active b∈Bkb \B_k. chosen nest (k=kik = k_i, ki≥0k_i \geq 0), using gλki=(1−Pki)Tki+(V‾ki−Vca)/λki2g_{\lambda_{k_i}} = (1-P_{k_i})T_{k_i} + (\bar{V}_{k_i} - V_{c_a})/\lambda_{k_i}^2: db(ki)=PkiTkiPib−𝟏b=caλki2+𝟏k(b)=kiP(b∣ki)[−PkiTki+Vib−V‾kiλki2(Pki−1+1λki)+1λki2] d^{(k_i)}_b = P_{k_i} T_{k_i} P_{ib} - \frac{\mathbf{1}_{b = c_a}}{\lambda_{k_i}^2} + \mathbf{1}_{k(b)=k_i} P(b \mid k_i) \left[   -P_{k_i} T_{k_i}   + \frac{V_{ib} - \bar{V}_{k_i}}{\lambda_{k_i}^2} \left(P_{k_i} - 1 + \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}}\right)   + \frac{1}{\lambda_{k_i}^2} \right] extra −1/λki2-1/\lambda_{k_i}^2 term b=cab = c_a arises differentiating (V‾ki−Vca)/λki2(\bar{V}_{k_i} - V_{c_a})/\lambda_{k_i}^2 component chosen-nest gradient.

what: 6.6 Consumer Surplus and the Nested Logsum
title: The math behind choicer: nested logit
text: nested logit, expected maximum utility (logsum) individual ii’s choice set replaces flat MNL log-sum two-level structure §2: logsumi=log⁡Di=log⁡(∑lexp(λllogIl)[+1]),log⁡Il=log⁡∑j∈Bl∩Ciexp⁡(Vijλl) \mathrm{logsum}_i = \log D_i = \log\left( \sum_{l} \exp\big(\lambda_l \log I_l\big) \; [+\, 1] \right), \qquad \log I_l = \log \sum_{j \B_l \cap C_i} \exp\!\left(\frac{V_{ij}}{\lambda_l}\right) +1=exp⁡(0)+1 = \exp(0) term appears outside option present singleton nests λl=1\lambda_l = 1 (§5.2). levels computed max-subtraction trick (§2.7). Two useful identities: Reduction MNL. λl=1\lambda_l = 1, λllog⁡Il=log⁡∑j∈Blexp⁡(Vij)\lambda_l \log I_l = \log \sum_{j \B_l} \exp(V_{ij}) nested logsum collapses MNL logsum log⁡∑jexp⁡(Vij)\log \sum_j \exp(V_{ij}). Outside-option link. Since Pi0=1/DiP_{i0} = 1 / D_i (§2.5), logsum equals −log⁡Pi0-\log P_{i0} — direct consistency check probability kernel. Expected consumer surplus follows Train (2009, Ch. 3) exactly MNL case: 𝔼[CSi]=logsumi−α \mathbb{E}[CS_i] = \frac{\mathrm{logsum}_i}{-\alpha} caveats: income effects, CS levels inherit ASC normalization, differences across scenarios (counterfactual newdata vs. baseline) meaningful. implementation reports point estimates NL (delta-method SE mean CS currently provided MNL ; Krinsky–Robb resampling coefficients practical interval method ). Code reference: R/surplus.R

Equal-shares null (default, exact unbalanced sets weights): ℓ0=−∑iwilog⁡(Mi+𝟏outside)\ell_0 = -\sum_i w_i \log(M_i + \mathbf{1}_{\text{outside}}). Market-shares null (constants-closed form): ℓ0=∑jNjlog⁡(sj)\ell_0 = \sum_j N_j \log(s_j); requires identical choice-set composition across individuals uniform weights. constants-model MNL — β=0\beta = 0 free ASCs nest structure undetermined — closed form applies. Hit rate: weighted share choice situations whose observed choice highest predicted NL probability; outside option outside good competes Pi0=1−∑jPijP_{i0} = 1 - \sum_j P_{ij}. Code reference: R/gof.R

what: choicer • fast discrete-choice models for economics
title: choicer • fast discrete-choice models for economics
text: choicer provides fast estimation discrete-choice models applied economics. Likelihoods, analytical gradients Hessians implemented C++ OpenMP parallelism, scaling efficiently specifications many alternative-specific constants. Post-estimation routines return predicted shares, - cross-price elasticities, diversion ratios, willingness--pay delta-method standard errors, goodness--fit measures, counterfactual predictions, BLP contraction. Supports multinomial logit (MNL), mixed logit (MXL), nested logit (NL); models added.

title: choicer • fast discrete-choice models for economics
text: Install released version CRAN: install development version GitHub:

title: choicer • fast discrete-choice models for economics
text: Estimate multinomial logit model compute elasticities diversion ratios: post-estimation toolkit available nested logit. Elasticities respect nest structure — within-nest cross-nest cross-elasticities differ, IIA hold across nests. blp() contraction NL accepts damping argument (default 1) can reduced models strong nesting:
code: library(choicer) library(data.table)  # Estimate fit <- run_mnlogit(     data = dt,     id_col = "id",     alt_col = "alt",     choice_col = "choice",     covariate_col = c("x1", "x2") )  summary(fit)  # Post-estimation predict(fit, type = "shares")        # predicted market shares elasticities(fit, elast_var = "x1")  # own- and cross-price elasticities diversion_ratios(fit)                # diversion ratio matrix wtp(fit, price_var = "x1")           # willingness-to-pay with delta-method SEs gof(fit)                             # McFadden R2 and hit rate (also in summary())  # Counterfactual / policy prediction: perturb the data and predict dt_cf <- copy(dt)[alt == 2, x1 := x1 + 1] predict(fit, type = "shares", newdata = dt_cf)  # Welfare: change in expected consumer surplus from the counterfactual cs0 <- consumer_surplus(fit, price_var = "x1") cs1 <- consumer_surplus(fit, price_var = "x1", newdata = dt_cf) cs1$mean_cs - cs0$mean_cs # Nested logit — simulate data and fit sim <- simulate_nl_data(N = 5e4, seed = 123)  fit_nl <- run_nestlogit(     data                   = sim$data,     id_col                 = "id",     alt_col                = "j",     choice_col             = "choice",     covariate_cols         = c("X", "W"),     nest_col               = "nest",     use_asc                = TRUE,     include_outside_option = TRUE,     outside_opt_label      = 0L,     keep_data              = TRUE   # required for post-estimation )  # Post-estimation predict(fit_nl, type = "shares")        # predicted market shares elasticities(fit_nl, elast_var = "X")   # J×J elasticity matrix (nest-consistent) diversion_ratios(fit_nl)                # J×J diversion matrix # BLP share inversion: recover mean utilities matching target shares target_shares <- predict(fit_nl, type = "shares") blp(fit_nl, target_shares, damping = 0.5)  # use damping < 1 for strongly-nested models

title: choicer • fast discrete-choice models for economics
text: fitted models support summary(), coef(), vcov(), logLik(), AIC(), BIC(), nobs(). summary() reports McFadden R2 hit rate alongside usual fit statistics, predict() accepts newdata (long data.frame modified design list) counterfactual policy prediction, even fits keep_data = FALSE.

Expected consumer surplus — consumer_surplus
text: Computes expected consumer surplus per choice situation (Train 2009, Ch. 3): $$E[CS_i] = \frac{logsum_i}{-\alpha},$$ \(logsum_i\) expected maximum utility (see logsum) \(\alpha\) (fixed) price coefficient, \(-\alpha\) marginal utility income. formula assumes income effects: utility linear price, marginal utility income constant across price changes considered.

title: Expected consumer surplus — consumer_surplus
code: consumer_surplus(   object,   price_var,   newdata = NULL,   level = 0.95,   weights = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'choicer_mnl' consumer_surplus(   object,   price_var,   newdata = NULL,   level = 0.95,   weights = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'choicer_mxl' consumer_surplus(   object,   price_var,   newdata = NULL,   level = 0.95,   weights = NULL,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'choicer_nl' consumer_surplus(   object,   price_var,   newdata = NULL,   level = 0.95,   weights = NULL,   ... )

title: Expected consumer surplus — consumer_surplus
text: object fitted model object (choicer_mnl, choicer_mxl, choicer_nl). price_var Name price variable. Must fixed-coefficient variable (column design matrix X). newdata Optional counterfactual data (data.frame list), logsum predict(). NULL (default), data stored fit time used (requires keep_data = TRUE). level Confidence level normal-approximation interval around mean CS (MNL ). Default 0.95. weights Optional numeric vector one weight per choice situation, used mean CS (SE), predict(): data.frame newdata, one weight per id order first appearance. Defaults equal weights. Ignored newdata NULL (stored fit weights apply). ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Expected consumer surplus — consumer_surplus
text: choicer_cs object: list cs (per-choice- situation surplus, length N), mean_cs (weighted mean), se_mean_cs (delta-method SE; NA MXL/NL variance-covariance matrix unavailable), ci (confidence interval mean), price_var, level, n.

title: Expected consumer surplus — consumer_surplus
text: Consumer surplus levels inherit additive utility normalization (particular ASC normalization), level defined constant; differences CS scenarios — e.g. consumer_surplus(fit, "price", newdata = scenario) minus baseline — economically meaningful quantity. MNL fits, delta-method standard error weighted mean CS reported (weights stored fit weights, resolved newdata weights). MXL NL fits point estimates returned (se_mean_cs = NA): delta method simulated MXL logsum nested logsum deferred; simulation-based intervals (Krinsky-Robb: resample coefficients asymptotic distribution recompute mean CS) practical alternative. price variable must fixed coefficient. mixed logit random price coefficient rejected (wtp): random denominator \(1/(-\alpha)\) generally finite moments.

title: Expected consumer surplus — consumer_surplus
text: Train, K. (2009). Discrete Choice Methods Simulation, 2nd ed., Ch. 3. Cambridge University Press.

title: Expected consumer surplus — consumer_surplus
code: # \donttest{ library(data.table) sim <- simulate_mnl_data(N = 1000, J = 3, beta = c(0.8, -0.6), seed = 123,                          outside_option = FALSE, vary_choice_set = FALSE) fit <- run_mnlogit(sim$data, "id", "alt", "choice", c("x1", "x2")) #> Optimization run time 0h:0m:0s  # treat x2 as the price variable cs0 <- consumer_surplus(fit, price_var = "x2") cs0 #> Consumer surplus, price variable: 'x2' #>   Mean CS: 1.692  #>   SE (delta method): 0.2281  #>   95% CI: [1.245, 2.139] #>   N: 1000  #> Note: CS levels depend on the utility (ASC) normalization; differences between scenarios are the meaningful quantity.  # Change in consumer surplus from a price increase on alternative 2: # levels depend on the ASC normalization, differences do not. dt_cf <- copy(sim$data)[alt == 2, x2 := x2 + 0.5] cs1 <- consumer_surplus(fit, price_var = "x2", newdata = dt_cf) delta_cs <- cs1$mean_cs - cs0$mean_cs delta_cs  # negative: the price increase lowers expected surplus #> [1] -0.07643608 # }

title: Goodness of fit for a fitted choice model — gof
text: choicer_gof object: list loglik, loglik_null, null, mcfadden_r2, mcfadden_r2_adj, hit_rate, nobs, n_params.

title: Goodness of fit for a fitted choice model — gof
text: Two null models available pseudo R-squared \(R^2 = 1 - LL / LL_0\) (adjusted: \(R^2_{adj} = 1 - (LL - K) / LL_0\) \(K\) number estimated parameters): "equal_shares" (default): every alternative individual \(\)'s choice set equally likely, \(LL_0 = -\sum_i w_i \log(M_i + 1_{outside})\). exact unbalanced choice sets arbitrary weights. "market_shares": maximized log-likelihood ASC-model, \(LL_0 = \sum_j N_j \log(s_j)\) \(N_j\) choice counts \(s_j\) observed market shares (including outside option present). closed form valid balanced choice sets uniform weights; otherwise error suggests refitting ASC-model. hit rate weighted share individuals whose observed choice highest predicted probability. model includes outside option, outside good competes predicted maximum (probability \(1 - \sum_j p_{ij}\)), individual predicted choose outside good hit actually . null log-likelihood hit rate require stored estimation data; models fitted keep_data = FALSE return NA fields message.

title: Goodness of fit for a fitted choice model — gof

what: Expected logsum (inclusive value) per choice situation — logsum
title: Expected logsum (inclusive value) per choice situation — logsum
text: Computes expected maximum utility ("logsum" inclusive value) choice situation, additive constant extreme-value error: MNL: \(\log \sum_j \exp(V_{ij})\). MXL: \(E_\beta[\log \sum_j \exp(V_{ij}(\beta))]\), simulated averaging log-sum-exp across deterministic Halton draws (regenerated object$draws_info). Taking log-sum-exp draw-averaged utilities understate expectation (Jensen's inequality), dedicated kernel (mxl_logsum) used. NL: \(\log \sum_b \exp(\lambda_b I_{ib})\) nest inclusive value \(I_{ib} = \log \sum_{j \b} \exp(V_{ij}/\lambda_b)\) (singleton nests \(\lambda_b = 1\)). model includes outside option, normalized utility \(V = 0\) contributes \(\exp(0)\) term sum.

title: Expected logsum (inclusive value) per choice situation — logsum

what: Asymptotic diagnostics for a Monte Carlo study — mc_asymptotics
title: Asymptotic diagnostics for a Monte Carlo study — mc_asymptotics
text: Consumes choicer_mc object returns per-parameter asymptotic diagnostics: Monte Carlo bias (MC standard error), empirical SD estimates, mean reported standard errors, SE--SD ratio (information-matrix-equality check), Wald coverage nominal 90 / 95 / 99 percent Wilson confidence bands, moments studentized statistic z = (theta_hat - theta_0) / se, four normality tests z (Shapiro-Wilk, Anderson-Darling via goftest::ad.test, hand-coded Jarque-Bera statistic, one-sample Kolmogorov-Smirnov test N(0, 1)).

title: Asymptotic diagnostics for a Monte Carlo study — mc_asymptotics
code: mc_asymptotics(   mc,   level = 0.95,   se_col = "se",   conv_threshold = 0.99,   se_ratio_threshold_floor = 0.1 )

title: Asymptotic diagnostics for a Monte Carlo study — mc_asymptotics
text: mc choicer_mc object returned monte_carlo(). level Confidence level Wilson bands coverage rates. Defaults 0.95. se_col Name column mc$replications use standard-error source. Defaults "se" (Hessian-based SE stored monte_carlo()). Callers augment replications alternative SE flavor (e.g., "se_bhhh" BHHH/OPG comparison) can pass column name recompute every SE-dependent diagnostic (mean_se, se_ratio, mean_se_w, cov90/95/99, z-moments, normality tests, pass flags) flavor. Useful information-matrix-equality check Claim 4 MXL validation suite. conv_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Minimum fraction replications must converge per-parameter pass_convergence flag TRUE. flag compares R_used / R_total (per parameter) threshold. Defaults 0.99. se_ratio_threshold_floor Numeric scalar. Minimum half-width pass_se_ratio band. actual band used max(se_ratio_threshold_floor, 3 * 1.4 / sqrt(R_used)), 1.4 / sqrt(R) term approximates large-sample SD mean_se / sd_emp. floor guarantees band never tighter historical hard cutoff. Defaults 0.10.

title: Asymptotic diagnostics for a Monte Carlo study — mc_asymptotics
text: object class choicer_mc_asymptotics — data.table one row per unique parameter columns documented — meta attached attribute (attr(x, "meta")).

title: Asymptotic diagnostics for a Monte Carlo study — mc_asymptotics
text: Six logical pass / fail flags attached every parameter row: pass_bias requires |bias_mc_se| < 3; pass_se_ratio requires |se_ratio - 1| lie within max(se_ratio_threshold_floor, 3 * 1.4 / sqrt(R_used)) (noise-aware band widens small R_used tightens floor large R_used); pass_cov95 requires nominal 95 percent level lie Wilson band empirical coverage; pass_skew requires |skew_z| < 0.3; pass_kurt requires excess kurtosis z [-0.5, 1.0]; pass_convergence requires per-parameter convergence rate (R_used / R_total) meet conv_threshold. Non-converged replications excluded per parameter (reported R_excluded). Winsorized (5 percent / 95 percent) versions bias, sd_emp, mean_se reported parallel columns (bias_w, sd_emp_w, mean_se_w) silent outlier exclusion transparent reader. Two robust SE--SD ratios accompany Hessian-mean-based se_ratio: se_ratio_med (median SE divided empirical SD) se_ratio_w (winsorized mean SE divided winsorized empirical SD); stay near 1 1-2 replications produce near-singular Hessians inflate mean_se. companion se_med column reports median per-replication SE used se_ratio_med. Neither robust ratio drives pass_* flag — purely informational. Winsorized z-moment counterparts (mean_z_w, sd_z_w, skew_z_w, kurt_excess_z_w) reported alongside raw z-moments feed additional pass_z_w flag (Winsorized skew within band pass_skew Winsorized excess kurtosis within band pass_kurt). companion pass_cov95_w flag TRUE either pass_cov95 TRUE per-rep Winsorized z-CI (empirical 2.5 / 97.5 percentiles Winsorized z) covers truth-zero. two flags designed boundary scenarios (e.g., near-zero variance components) small number reps vanishing SE inflate raw z-moments without indicating estimator defect.

what: Gibbs sampler for the Bayesian multinomial probit model — mnp_gibbs
title: Gibbs sampler for the Bayesian multinomial probit model — mnp_gibbs
text: Runs McCulloch-Rossi (1994) Gibbs sampler Albert-Chib data augmentation multinomial probit model utility differences base alternative. chain operates non-identified parameterization (unrestricted Sigma); identified quantities obtained normalizing draw sigma_11 (handled run_mnprobit).

title: Gibbs sampler for the Bayesian multinomial probit model — mnp_gibbs
code: mnp_gibbs(X, y, p, beta_bar, A, nu, V, R, burn, thin, seed, trace = 0L)

title: Gibbs sampler for the Bayesian multinomial probit model — mnp_gibbs
text: X (N*p) x K stacked design matrix utility differences. Rows grouped choice situation, p = J - 1 difference rows situation ordered alternative. y N vector choices: 0 base alternative, j 1..p j-th non-base alternative. p Number utility differences (J - 1). beta_bar K vector, prior mean beta. K x K prior precision matrix beta. nu Inverse-Wishart prior degrees freedom (>= p). V p x p inverse-Wishart prior scale matrix. R Total number Gibbs iterations. burn Number initial iterations discard (0 <= burn < R). thin Keep every thin-th post-burn-draw. seed Master RNG seed (non-negative; streams derive ). trace Print progress every trace iterations (0 = silent).

title: Gibbs sampler for the Bayesian multinomial probit model — mnp_gibbs

Intercity travel mode choice — mode_choice
text: Stated choices intercity travel mode 210 travellers, choosing among four modes: air, train, bus car. classic Greene & Hensher (1997) data set, reshaped choicer's long layout (one row per traveller--alternative). convenient, recognizable example multinomial nested logit models demand/welfare toolkit (elasticities, diversion ratios, willingness--pay, counterfactuals).

title: Intercity travel mode choice — mode_choice
Intercity travel mode choice — mode_choice
text: data frame 840 rows (210 travellers x 4 modes) 9 columns: id Integer traveller (choice situation) identifier, 1-210. mode Factor giving travel mode: "air", "train", "bus" "car". Use alternative column. choice Integer indicator, 1 chosen mode 0 otherwise. Exactly one mode chosen per traveller. wait Terminal waiting time minutes (0 car). travel -vehicle travel time minutes. vcost -vehicle cost component, currency units. gcost Generalized cost measure, currency units. income Household income (traveller level, thousands). size Size travelling party (traveller level).

title: Intercity travel mode choice — mode_choice
text: Greene, W. H. Hensher, D. . (1997). Reshaped TravelMode data distributed AER package (https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=AER). data appear Greene's Econometric Analysis several choice-modelling packages.

title: Intercity travel mode choice — mode_choice
text: wait, travel, vcost gcost vary across modes within traveller, income size traveller-level attributes constant across modes. standard specification regresses choice wait, travel vcost alternative-specific constants; vcost plays role price willingness--pay consumer-surplus calculations.

title: Monte Carlo parameter recovery — monte_carlo
text: Replicates (DGP -> fit) cycle R times independent seeds collects per-parameter estimates, standard errors, bias, coverage. Returns choicer_mc object; call summary() aggregated statistics (mean estimate, bias, RMSE, coverage rate, convergence rate).

title: Monte Carlo parameter recovery — monte_carlo
code: monte_carlo(   sim_fun,   fit_fun,   R = 100,   seed = 1L,   parallel = FALSE,   progress = TRUE,   ... )

title: Monte Carlo parameter recovery — monte_carlo
text: sim_fun Function seed returning choicer_sim. fit_fun Function choicer_sim returning choicer_fit. R Number replications. seed Base integer seed. Replication r uses seed + r - 1L. parallel Logical; TRUE future.apply available, run replications parallel using user's active future::plan(). progress Logical; print one-line progress update per iteration serial mode. Ignored parallel = TRUE. ... Unused.

title: Monte Carlo parameter recovery — monte_carlo
text: choicer_mc object: list elements replications (long data.table one row per estimated parameter per replication) meta (run metadata).

title: Monte Carlo parameter recovery — monte_carlo
text: iteration calls sim_fun(seed = seed + r - 1L), fit_fun(sim). Write sim_fun closure captures N, J, DGP settings forwards seed. Write fit_fun closure takes choicer_sim returns fitted choicer_fit object, wrapping data-preparation, draws, optimizer-control setup.

what: BHHH (outer product of gradients) information matrix for Mixed Logit — mxl_bhhh_parallel
title: BHHH (outer product of gradients) information matrix for Mixed Logit — mxl_bhhh_parallel
text: Computes BHHH approximation observed information matrix Mixed Logit model: \(H_{BHHH} = \sum_i w_i \cdot s_i s_i^\top\), \(s_i\) per-individual score (gradient \(\log \bar{P}_i\)). outer product gradients (OPG) estimator provides alternative analytical Hessian standard error computation scales large problems analytical Hessian infeasible (e.g., many alternatives simulation draws).

title: BHHH (outer product of gradients) information matrix for Mixed Logit — mxl_bhhh_parallel
code: mxl_bhhh_parallel(   theta,   X,   W,   alt_idx,   choice_idx,   M,   weights,   eta_draws,   rc_dist,   rc_correlation = TRUE,   rc_mean = FALSE,   use_asc = TRUE,   include_outside_option = FALSE,   gen_seed = -1L,   gen_scramble = 1L,   gen_S = 0L )

title: BHHH (outer product of gradients) information matrix for Mixed Logit — mxl_bhhh_parallel
text: theta vector collecting model parameters (beta, mu, L, delta (ASCs)) X design matrix covariates fixed coefficients; sum(M_i) x K_x W design matrix covariates random coefficients; sum(M_i) x K_w J x K_w alt_idx sum(M) x 1 vector indices alternatives within choice set; 1-based indexing choice_idx N x 1 vector indices chosen alternatives; 1-based indexing relative X; 0 used include_outside_option=True M N x 1 vector number alternatives individual weights N x 1 vector weights observation eta_draws Array choice situation draws; K_w x S x N rc_dist K_w x 1 integer vector indicating distribution random coefficients: 0 = normal, 1 = log-normal rc_correlation whether random coefficients correlated rc_mean whether estimate means random coefficients. use_asc whether use alternative-specific constants. include_outside_option whether include outside option normalized 0 (, outside option included data) gen_seed Integer master seed --fly Halton generator. < 0 (default) uses materialized eta_draws cube; >= 0 generates draws fly seed. gen_scramble Integer scramble mode --fly generation: 0 = identity permutations (plain Halton, compat), 1 = Owen (2017) digit scrambling. gen_S Integer number draws per individual, used gen_seed >= 0.

title: BHHH (outer product of gradients) information matrix for Mixed Logit — mxl_bhhh_parallel
text: n_params x n_params PSD matrix representing observed information matrix estimated outer product gradients (sign convention negated Hessian returned mxl_hessian_parallel, can inverted directly obtain vcov).

title: BHHH (outer product of gradients) information matrix for Mixed Logit — mxl_bhhh_parallel
text: BHHH/OPG estimator asymptotically equivalent Hessian-based information matrix true MLE. finite samples can underestimate standard errors, particularly model mis-specified away optimum.

title: BHHH (outer product of gradients) information matrix for Mixed Logit — mxl_bhhh_parallel

what: BLP contraction mapping for mixed logit — mxl_blp_contraction
title: BLP contraction mapping for mixed logit — mxl_blp_contraction

title: Simulated expected logsum (inclusive value) for Mixed Logit — mxl_logsum
text: Computes simulated expected logsum (expected maximum utility, additive constant) choice situation: $$logsum_i = (1/S) \sum_s \log \sum_j \exp(V_{ij}^s),$$ inner sum runs individual 's alternatives includes outside option's \(\exp(0)\) term include_outside_option = TRUE. log-sum-exp must averaged across draws: applying log-sum-exp draw-averaged utilities returned mxl_predict understates expectation log-sum-exp convex (Jensen's inequality).

K: Number columns X. alt_mapping: Data.table mapping alternatives summary statistics (base alternative alt_int = 1). base_alt: Resolved label base alternative. param_map: Named list integer index vectors (beta, asc). use_asc: Logical flag. dropped_cols: Names columns dropped due collinearity, . data_spec: List column name metadata.

Parameter recovery table — recovery_table
text: Compares fitted coefficients set true parameter values scale estimator's internal parameterization. Returns one row per estimated parameter true value, estimate, standard error, bias, relative bias (%), z-score truth, Wald CI, coverage indicator.

code: recovery_table(object, truth = NULL, level = 0.95, ...)  # S3 method for class 'choicer_fit' recovery_table(object, truth = NULL, level = 0.95, ...)  # S3 method for class 'choicer_mnp' recovery_table(object, truth = NULL, level = 0.95, ...)  # S3 method for class 'choicer_mc' recovery_table(object, truth = NULL, level = 0.95, ...)

title: Parameter recovery table — recovery_table
text: object choicer_fit object (MNL, MXL, NL) choicer_mc result. truth Either choicer_sim object (whose $true_params used) named list true parameter values. level Confidence level Wald CI coverage indicator. Default 0.95. ... Unused.

title: Parameter recovery table — recovery_table
text: See class-specific methods.

title: Parameter recovery table — recovery_table
text: MXL fits sigma block compares raw Cholesky parameters (L_params), reconstructed covariance matrix. log-normal random-coefficient means raw mu estimate compared directly; callers want recovery DGP scale (exp(mu)) transform sides calling. estimator normalized first inside alternative's ASC zero (happens MNL/MXL include_outside_option = FALSE outside option baked fit), first entry truth$delta dropped comparison lengths match.

title: Parameter recovery table — recovery_table
text: recovery_table(choicer_fit): Returns choicer_recovery object (data.table) columns parameter, group, true, estimate, se, bias, rel_bias_pct, z_vs_true, lower_ci, upper_ci, covers. recovery_table(choicer_mnp): Method Bayesian MNP fits (choicer_mnp). estimate column holds posterior means identified draws se holds posterior standard deviations, lower_ci / upper_ci normal-approximation credible intervals. addition beta asc blocks, sigma block compares identified covariance utility differences (lower triangle estimator's row-major Sigma_ij order); first row sigma_11 = 1 normalization exact construction. truth must identified scale, returned simulate_mnp_data(). recovery_table(choicer_mc): choicer_mc object, delegates summary(object, level) returns choicer_mc_summary. Inspect object$replications directly per-rep detail.

title: Parameter recovery table — recovery_table
