title: Runs multinomial logit estimation — run_mnlogit
text: Estimates multinomial logit model via maximum likelihood.

title: Runs multinomial logit estimation — run_mnlogit
code: run_mnlogit(   data = NULL,   id_col = NULL,   alt_col = NULL,   choice_col = NULL,   covariate_cols = NULL,   input_data = NULL,   optimizer = NULL,   control = list(),   weights = NULL,   outside_opt_label = NULL,   include_outside_option = FALSE,   use_asc = TRUE,   keep_data = TRUE,   scale_vars = c("none", "sd", "mad", "iqr"),   nloptr_opts = NULL )

title: Runs multinomial logit estimation — run_mnlogit
text: data Data frame containing choice data (convenience workflow). Mutually exclusive input_data. id_col Name column identifying choice situations (individuals). alt_col Name column identifying alternatives. choice_col Name column indicating chosen alternative (1 = chosen, 0 = chosen). covariate_cols Vector names columns used covariates. input_data List output prepare_mnl_data (advanced workflow). Mutually exclusive data. optimizer Optimizer use: "nloptr" (default), "optim", custom function signature f(theta_init, eval_f, lower, upper, control) eval_f(theta) returns list(objective, gradient). Must return list par/value (solution/objective). custom function accepts control ..., control argument forwarded; otherwise silently ignored. control List optimizer-specific control parameters passed chosen optimizer (e.g., list(maxeval = 2000) nloptr). weights Optional vector weights choice situation. NULL, equal weights used. outside_opt_label Label outside option (). NULL, outside option assumed. include_outside_option Logical indicating whether include outside option model. use_asc Logical indicating whether include alternative-specific constants (ASCs) model. keep_data Logical. TRUE (default), stores prepared data returned object predict() post-estimation functions. scale_vars Pre-estimation column scaling design matrix. One "none" (default), "sd" (sample standard deviation), "mad" (stats::mad), "iqr" (stats::IQR(x) / 1.349). "none", every column X divided chosen scale optimization improve Hessian conditioning. Coefficients standard errors back-transformed user's natural units via delta method, reported quantities invariant choice. nloptr_opts Deprecated. Use optimizer control instead.

title: Runs multinomial logit estimation — run_mnlogit
text: choicer_mnl object (inherits choicer_fit). Standard S3 methods available: summary(), coef(), vcov(), logLik(), AIC(), BIC(), nobs(), predict().

title: Runs multinomial logit estimation — run_mnlogit
text: Two workflows supported: Convenience (default) Supply data column names. Data preparation (prepare_mnl_data) handled automatically. Advanced Call prepare_mnl_data pass result via input_data.

title: Runs multinomial logit estimation — run_mnlogit

what: Runs Bayesian multinomial probit estimation — run_mnprobit
title: Runs Bayesian multinomial probit estimation — run_mnprobit
text: Estimates multinomial probit model Gibbs sampling data augmentation (Albert & Chib 1993; McCulloch & Rossi 1994). model specified utility differences base alternative: choice situation \(\) \(J\) alternatives, \(w_i = X_i \beta + \epsilon_i\) \(\epsilon_i \sim N_{J-1}(0, \Sigma)\).

title: Runs Bayesian multinomial probit estimation — run_mnprobit
code: run_mnprobit(   data = NULL,   id_col = NULL,   alt_col = NULL,   choice_col = NULL,   covariate_cols = NULL,   input_data = NULL,   base_alt = NULL,   use_asc = TRUE,   prior = list(),   mcmc = list(),   keep_data = TRUE )

title: Runs Bayesian multinomial probit estimation — run_mnprobit
text: data Data frame containing choice data (convenience workflow). Mutually exclusive input_data. id_col Name column identifying choice situations (individuals). alt_col Name column identifying alternatives. choice_col Name column indicating chosen alternative (1 = chosen, 0 = chosen). covariate_cols Vector names columns used covariates. input_data List output prepare_mnp_data (advanced workflow). Mutually exclusive data. base_alt Label base (reference) alternative used utility differencing. NULL (default), first alternative sort order used. use_asc Logical indicating whether include alternative-specific constants (one intercept per non-base alternative differenced utilities). prior Named list prior settings, merged defaults: beta_bar Prior mean \(\beta\) (default rep(0, K)). Prior precision \(\beta\) (default 0.01 * diag(K)). nu Inverse-Wishart degrees freedom (default p + 3). V Inverse-Wishart scale matrix (default nu * diag(p)). mcmc Named list MCMC settings, merged defaults: R Total Gibbs iterations (default 10000). burn Burn-iterations discarded (default floor(R / 5)). thin Keep every thin-th post-burn-draw (default 1). seed Master RNG seed (default: drawn R's RNG). trace Print progress every trace iterations (default 0, silent). keep_data Logical. TRUE (default), stores prepared data returned object.

title: Runs Bayesian multinomial probit estimation — run_mnprobit
text: choicer_mnp object. S3 methods available: summary(), coef() (posterior means identified coefficients), vcov() (posterior covariance identified coefficient draws), nobs(). Posterior draws stored $draws (beta / sigma identified scale, beta_raw / sigma_raw unnormalized).

title: Runs Bayesian multinomial probit estimation — run_mnprobit
text: Two workflows supported: Convenience (default) Supply data column names. Data preparation (prepare_mnp_data) handled automatically. Advanced Call prepare_mnp_data pass result via input_data. Identification. multinomial probit likelihood invariant common rescaling \((\beta, \Sigma) \(c\beta, c^2\Sigma)\). sampler runs non-identified parameterization (unrestricted \(\Sigma\) inverse-Wishart prior) identified quantities computed normalizing kept draw \(\sigma_{11}\): \(\beta / \sqrt{\sigma_{11}}\) \(\Sigma / \sigma_{11}\). McCulloch & Rossi (1994) default, keeps Gibbs conditionals conjugate mixes better fully identified sampler McCulloch, Polson & Rossi (2000). Reported coefficients, standard deviations, credible intervals posterior summaries identified draws. Reproducibility. sampler uses thread-safe RNG one stream per (iteration, observation), results reproducible independent number OpenMP threads (see set_num_threads()). mcmc$seed supplied, master seed drawn R's RNG, set.seed() controls run. Scope. Balanced choice sets required: every choice situation must contain \(J\) alternatives. model outside option, include explicit rows zero covariates set base_alt label.

title: Runs mixed logit estimation — run_mxlogit
code: run_mxlogit(   data = NULL,   id_col = NULL,   alt_col = NULL,   choice_col = NULL,   covariate_cols = NULL,   random_var_cols = NULL,   input_data = NULL,   eta_draws = NULL,   S = 100L,   rc_dist = NULL,   rc_mean = FALSE,   rc_correlation = FALSE,   use_asc = TRUE,   theta_init = NULL,   lower = NULL,   upper = NULL,   optimizer = NULL,   control = list(),   se_method = c("hessian", "bhhh", "sandwich"),   scale_vars = c("none", "sd", "mad", "iqr"),   weights = NULL,   outside_opt_label = NULL,   include_outside_option = FALSE,   draws = c("store", "generate"),   seed = NULL,   scramble = c("owen", "none"),   keep_data = TRUE,   nloptr_opts = NULL,   weights_col = NULL )

title: Runs mixed logit estimation — run_mxlogit
text: data Data frame containing choice data (convenience workflow). Mutually exclusive input_data. id_col Name column identifying choice situations. alt_col Name column identifying alternatives. choice_col Name column indicating chosen alternative (1/0). covariate_cols Vector column names fixed covariates. random_var_cols Vector column names random coefficients. input_data List output prepare_mxl_data (advanced workflow). Mutually exclusive data. eta_draws Array shape K_w x S x N standard normal draws. Required advanced workflow; auto-generated S convenience workflow. S Integer number Halton draws per individual (convenience workflow ). Default 100. rc_dist Integer vector indicating distribution random coefficients (0 = normal, 1 = log-normal). Default: normal. rc_mean Logical indicating whether estimate means random coefficients. rc_correlation Logical indicating whether random coefficients correlated (convenience workflow). Ignored input_data used (taken prepared data). use_asc Logical indicating whether include alternative-specific constants. theta_init Initial parameter vector natural-scale units. NULL, defaults zeros \(\beta\), \(\mu\), ASC blocks, log(0.5) Cholesky diagonal (diagonal factor \(L_{pp} = 0.5\), .e. moderate random-coefficient variance 0.25). zero--diagonal alternative corresponds \(L_{pp} = 1\) (unit RC variance), often lets first L-BFGS step overshoot. lower, upper Optional parameter bounds optimizer, natural-scale units (forward-transformed internally scaled space scale_vars != "none"). accepts three forms: NULL (default) Unbounded (-Inf/Inf). Unnamed numeric vector length n_params Full-length vector ordered exactly like theta_init (nloptr-native form). Named numeric vector Names must subset parameter names (\(\beta\) block: column names X; \(\mu\) block: Mu_<col> (rc_mean = TRUE); Cholesky block: L_<><j> \(\ge j\); ASC block: ASC_<level>). Unlisted parameters default \(\pm\infty\). recommended form typical use, e.g. lower = c(L_11 = -5, L_22 = -5) clip Cholesky diagonals. optimizer Optimizer use: "nloptr" (default), "optim", custom function. See run_mnlogit details. control List optimizer-specific control parameters. se_method Method computing standard errors. One "hessian" (default) analytical Hessian simulated log-likelihood, "bhhh" BHHH/outer-product--gradients (OPG) estimator, "sandwich" robust (Huber-White) variance \(V = ^{-1} B ^{-1}\) (bread \(\) = weighted negated Hessian, meat \(B\) = weight-squared OPG). Use "sandwich" valid inference choice-based / WESML weighting, inverse-Hessian ordinary BHHH invalid; reduces usual robust variance uniform weights. BHHH scales better large problems (many alternatives simulation draws) may underestimate standard errors finite samples away optimum. scale_vars Pre-estimation column scaling design matrices. One "none" (default), "sd" (sample standard deviation), "mad" (stats::mad, .e. 1.4826 \(\times\) median absolute deviation; SD-equivalent normality), "iqr" (stats::IQR(x) / 1.349; also SD-equivalent normality). "none", every column X W divided chosen scale optimization improve Hessian conditioning. Robust scales ("mad"/"iqr") better capture bulk heavy-tailed columns SD dominated outliers, stats::mad can return zero half column's entries identical (e.g., sparse 0/1 dummy) trigger near-constant-column error "sd". Coefficients standard errors back-transformed user's natural units via delta method, reported quantities invariant choice. Columns W associated log-normal random coefficients (rc_dist == 1) passed unchanged, since shifted log-normal parameterization admit closed-form back-transform multiplicative scaling. weights Optional weight vector (convenience workflow). NULL, equal weights used. outside_opt_label Label outside option (convenience workflow). include_outside_option Logical whether include outside option (convenience workflow). draws Draw storage mode. One "store" (default) "generate". "store" pre-materializes full \(K_w \times S \times N\) Halton cube (existing behavior, exact reproducibility). "generate" computes individual's draws --fly C++ stored seed, eliminating O(N) cube; recommended memory-constrained large-N settings. Uses randomized digit-scrambled (Owen 2017) Halton sequences scramble = "owen". supported convenience workflow. seed Integer master seed --fly generator. Used draws = "generate". NULL (default), seed drawn R's RNG call time (set.seed() governs reproducibility). Ignored draws = "store". scramble Scrambling mode --fly Halton draws. One "owen" (default) Owen (2017) digit scrambling, "none" plain unscrambled Halton (identity permutations). "none" reproduces randtoolbox sequence exactly intended testing; "owen" strongly recommended estimation \(K_w > 5\). Used draws = "generate". keep_data Logical. TRUE (default), stores prepared data returned object post-estimation functions. nloptr_opts Deprecated. Use optimizer control instead. weights_col Optional name column data holding per-row weight (constant within choice situation). Mutually exclusive weights; recommended way pass WESML weights sample_by_choice / wesml_weights, since alignment id rather position. Convenience workflow . data carries choice-based-sampling provenance ("choice_sampling" attribute, attached sample_by_choice / wesml_weights) neither weights weights_col supplied, recorded weight column auto-detected applied (message); column absent call errors rather silently fitting unweighted model WESML label.

TRUE (default), stores prepared data returned object post-estimation functions. se_method Method computing standard errors: "hessian" (default, analytical Hessian via nl_loglik_hessian_parallel) "numeric" (finite-difference oracle via nl_loglik_numeric_hessian). nloptr_opts Deprecated. Use optimizer control instead.

title: Simulate multinomial probit data — simulate_mnp_data
text: Generates synthetic choice data MNP data-generating process estimated run_mnprobit(): latent utility differences base alternative (alternative 1), $$w_i = X_i \beta + \delta + \varepsilon_i, \qquad       \varepsilon_i \sim N_{J-1}(0, \Sigma),$$ alternative \(j > 1\) chosen iff \(w_{ij} > \max(0, \max_{k \neq j} w_{ik})\) base chosen iff \(w_{ij} < 0\). Covariates Uniform(-1, 1). Choice sets balanced (every individual faces J alternatives), MNP estimator requires; outside-option flag — model outside good zero-covariate base alternative instead.

title: Simulate multinomial probit data — simulate_mnp_data
code: simulate_mnp_data(   N = 5000,   J = 3,   beta = c(0.8, -0.6),   delta = NULL,   Sigma = matrix(c(1, 0.5, 0.5, 1.5), nrow = 2),   seed = 123 )

title: Simulate multinomial probit data — simulate_mnp_data
text: N Number choice situations. J Number alternatives (alternative 1 base). beta Fixed coefficients x1..x{K_x} (length K_x = length(beta)). delta ASCs differenced utilities, one per non-base alternative (length J - 1). Defaults alternating pattern c(0.5, -0.5). Sigma Covariance matrix differenced errors ((J-1) x (J-1)). seed Random seed (NULL skips set.seed()).

title: Simulate multinomial probit data — simulate_mnp_data
text: choicer_sim object. true_params contains beta, delta, Sigma identified scale (see Details).

title: Simulate multinomial probit data — simulate_mnp_data
text: MNP likelihood identifies parameters scale, true_params reported identified scale (normalized \(\sigma_{11}\)): beta \(= \beta / \sqrt{\sigma_{11}}\), delta \(= \delta / \sqrt{\sigma_{11}}\), Sigma \(= \Sigma / \sigma_{11}\) — scale run_mnprobit() reports posterior. default Sigma (\(\sigma_{11} = 1\)) DGP scale identified scale coincide.

text: N Number choice situations. J Number inside alternatives. beta Fixed coefficients x1..x{K_x} (length K_x = length(beta)). delta ASCs inside alternatives (length J). Defaults alternating pattern c(0.5, -0.5). mu Mean shifter random coefficients (length K_w = ncol(Sigma)). Defaults zero vector. Sigma Covariance matrix random coefficients (square, K_w x K_w). rc_dist Integer vector (length K_w): 0L normal, 1L log-normal. Default NULL treated -normal. rc_correlation Logical; NULL (default) auto-detected -diagonal entries Sigma. price_cols Character vector w* column names draw -Uniform(0.1, 3) instead Uniform(-1, 1). Default NULL. seed Random seed (NULL skips set.seed()). outside_option Logical; include outside option alt = 0. vary_choice_set Logical; TRUE (default) choice set size sampled uniformly 2:J.

title: Simulate mixed logit data — simulate_mxl_data
text: choicer_sim object. true_params includes beta, delta, Sigma, L_params (packed Cholesky parameters), mu, rc_dist, rc_correlation.

title: Simulate mixed logit data — simulate_mxl_data
text: Random coefficients constructed match estimator's parameterization src/mxlogit.cpp. every dimension raw draw L %*% eta eta ~ N(0, ). normal random coefficient (rc_dist = 0) gamma_k = mu_k + (L %*% eta)_k. log-normal random coefficient (rc_dist = 1) follows shifted log-normal beta_k = exp(mu_k) + exp((L %*% eta)_k) – textbook exp(mu_k + sigma_k * eta) – mu_k true_params$mu scale estimator recovers recovery_table() can compare like--like.

what: Robust (sandwich) variance for a weighted / choice-based mixed logit fit — wesml_vcov
title: Robust (sandwich) variance for a weighted / choice-based mixed logit fit — wesml_vcov
text: Recomputes robust Huber-White sandwich variance \(V = ^{-1} B ^{-1}\) fitted mixed logit, bread \(= \sum_i w_i (-H_i)\) weighted negated Hessian meat \(B = \sum_i w_i^2 s_i s_i'\) weight-squared outer product per-individual scores. appropriate variance choice-based (endogenous stratified) / WESML weighting, inverse-Hessian ordinary BHHH variance invalid. can called fitted model (e.g. one estimated se_method = "hessian") obtain robust standard errors post hoc, without refitting.

what: WESML weights for choice-based (endogenous stratified) samples — wesml_weights
title: WESML weights for choice-based (endogenous stratified) samples — wesml_weights
text: Computes Manski-Lerman (1977) Weighted Exogenous Sample Maximum Likelihood (WESML) weights choice-based sample. weight choice situation whose chosen alternative \(j\) \(w = Q(j) / H(j)\), \(Q(j)\) population share alternative \(j\) \(H(j)\) sample share among choosers. Using weights run_mxlogit restores consistency choice-based sampling; pair se_method = "sandwich" valid (robust) standard errors (plain inverse-Hessian invalid weighting).

title: WESML weights for choice-based (endogenous stratified) samples — wesml_weights

title: WESML weights for choice-based (endogenous stratified) samples — wesml_weights

NULL (default) computed data fraction choice situations choosing alternative. normalize TRUE (default) returned weights scaled mean 1. affect point estimates sandwich variance. attach TRUE, return data row-level weight column appended (per-situation weight repeated across rows situation), ready pass run_mxlogit(weights_col = ...). FALSE (default) return id-keyed table weights. weight_name Name weight column (default ".wesml_weight"). outside_opt_label, include_outside_option Set include_outside_option = TRUE supply outside_opt_label outside good implicit (choice situations 1 choice_col treated chosen outside good).

Compute willingness to pay — wtp
text: Computes willingness--pay (WTP) ratios delta-method standard errors fitted choice model. attribute coefficient \(\theta_k\) price coefficient \(\theta_p\), WTP $$WTP_k = -\theta_k / \theta_p,$$ marginal rate substitution attribute price. Standard errors use delta method analytic gradients \(\partial g/\partial \theta_k = -1/\theta_p\) \(\partial g/\partial \theta_p = \theta_k/\theta_p^2\), applied corresponding 2x2 block vcov(object).

title: Compute willingness to pay — wtp
code: wtp(object, price_var, attr_vars = NULL, level = 0.95, ...)  # S3 method for class 'choicer_fit' wtp(object, price_var, attr_vars = NULL, level = 0.95, ...)  # S3 method for class 'choicer_mxl' wtp(object, price_var, attr_vars = NULL, level = 0.95, ...)

title: Compute willingness to pay — wtp
text: object fitted model object (choicer_mnl, choicer_mxl, choicer_nl). price_var Name price variable. Must fixed-coefficient variable (column design matrix X). attr_vars Character vector attributes report. Defaults fixed-coefficient variables price_var (plus, mixed logit rc_mean = TRUE, random coefficients). ASC names (e.g. "ASC_2") may also supplied; WTP ASC \(-ASC_j / \theta_p\). level Confidence level normal-approximation interval \(Estimate \pm z_{1-(1-level)/2} \times SE\). Default 0.95. ... Additional arguments passed methods.

title: Compute willingness to pay — wtp
text: data.frame class choicer_wtp one row per attribute columns Estimate, Std_Error, z_value, CI_lower, CI_upper. Attributes price_var level record inputs; median_rows lists rows median (rather mean) WTP. Standard errors NA variance-covariance matrix unavailable.

title: Compute willingness to pay — wtp
text: mixed logit models, random coefficients included via estimated location parameters. package's log-normal random coefficient shifted log-normal \(\beta_k = \exp(\mu_k) + \exp((L\eta)_k)\) (see run_mxlogit()), : Normal random coefficient \(k\) (rc_mean = TRUE): mean WTP \(-\mu_k / \theta_p\), labeled Mu_x. Log-normal random coefficient \(k\) (rc_mean = TRUE): median WTP \(-(\exp(\mu_k) + 1) / \theta_p\), since median \(\exp((L\eta)_k)\) 1. (mean, \(\exp(\mu_k) + \exp(\sigma_k^2/2)\), highly sensitive estimated variance; median robust summary.) rows labeled attribute name flagged medians printed. Log-normal random coefficient rc_mean = FALSE: \(\beta_k = \exp((L\eta)_k)\) median 1, median WTP \(-1/\theta_p\) uncertainty driven solely \(\theta_p\). Normal random coefficients rc_mean = FALSE mean 0 construction excluded table. price variable must fixed coefficient. random price coefficient rejected: ratio two random coefficients generally finite moments (denominator positive density 0), mean median WTP computed location parameters meaningless. Use fixed price coefficient, estimate model WTP space.

title: Compute willingness to pay — wtp
code: # \donttest{ library(data.table) sim <- simulate_mnl_data(N = 1000, J = 4, beta = c(0.8, -0.6), seed = 123,                          outside_option = FALSE, vary_choice_set = FALSE) fit <- run_mnlogit(sim$data, "id", "alt", "choice", c("x1", "x2")) #> Optimization run time 0h:0m:0s # treat x2 as the price variable wtp(fit, price_var = "x2") #> Willingness to pay (WTP), price variable: 'x2' (95% CI) #>    Estimate Std_Error z_value CI_lower CI_upper #> x1     1.59    0.2336   6.809    1.132    2.048 wtp(fit, price_var = "x2", attr_vars = c("x1", "ASC_2"), level = 0.90) #> Willingness to pay (WTP), price variable: 'x2' (90% CI) #>       Estimate Std_Error z_value CI_lower CI_upper #> x1       1.590    0.2336   6.809    1.206    1.974 #> ASC_2   -2.027    0.3157  -6.420   -2.546   -1.507 # }

title: choicer (development version)
text: Added five vignettes: getting-started tour (“Discrete choice data policy, dozen lines”) plus one per model (multinomial logit, mixed logit, nested logit, Bayesian multinomial probit). Added mode_choice data set: classic Greene & Hensher intercity travel-mode choice data (210 travellers x 4 modes), choicer’s long layout, used getting-started vignette. Added pkgdown website configuration, including model derivation notes “math behind choicer” articles.

what: Mixed logit — on-the-fly randomized Halton draws
title: choicer (development version)
text: draws = "store" (default) keeps existing behavior: full K_w × S × N Halton cube pre-materialized stored memory. draws = "generate" activates new mode: individual’s S draws computed fly C++ compact seed, eliminating O(N) eta_draws cube. recommended choice N large memory constrained. scramble = "owen" (default draws = "generate") applies Owen (2017) digit scrambling Halton sequence better high-dimensional uniformity; recommended K_w > 5 (Bhat 2003). scramble = "none" reproduces randtoolbox sequence exactly intended testing. seed sets integer master seed --fly generator; NULL (default), seed drawn R’s RNG set.seed() governs reproducibility. Ignored draws = "store". draws_info field choicer_mxl objects gains three new elements: mode ("store" "generate"), seed, scramble. Existing code accesses draws_info$S, draws_info$N, draws_info$K_w unaffected; new fields NULL objects fitted release. post-estimation generics (predict(), elasticities(), diversion_ratios(), logsum(), consumer_surplus(), vcov()) propagate fitted draw mode automatically — user action required. Default behavior (draws = "store") bitwise unchanged.

title: choicer (development version)
text: run_mnprobit() — Bayesian multinomial probit via Gibbs sampling data augmentation (Albert & Chib 1993; McCulloch & Rossi 1994). Runs non-identified chain conjugate priors reports identified quantities normalized per draw sigma_11. New choicer_mnp posterior object summary() (posterior mean, SD, credible intervals), coef(), vcov(), nobs(); math note docs/bayesian_multinomial_probit_math.md C++ MCMC infrastructure built scratch (src/rng.h, src/bayes_samplers.h): xoshiro256++/splitmix64 RNG one stream per (iteration, observation) — draws bitwise reproducible independent OpenMP thread count — plus exact truncated-normal, multivariate-normal, Wishart (Bartlett), inverse-Wishart samplers. truncated normal picks cheapest exact method per region (naive normal rejection high-mass regions, Robert 1995 exponential rejection tail, inverse CDF narrow intervals) Gibbs chain runs inside single persistent OpenMP region: latent-utility sweep mean refresh work-shared across choice situations, conjugate beta/Sigma conditionals run master thread lightweight barriers hand-rolled fixed-order linear algebra (BLAS inside region), truncated-normal conditional moments hoisted per Sigma draw. _benchmarks/ MNP preset samples ~2x faster MNP::mnp() ~3.5x faster bayesm::rmnpGibbs() one thread, scales threads top simulate_mnp_data() — probit DGP returning choicer_sim truth identified scale; recovery_table() gains choicer_mnp method (posterior mean/SD, normal-approximation credible intervals, sigma block); parameter-recovery walkthrough inst/simulations/mnp_simulation.R

title: choicer (development version)
text: wtp() — willingness--pay MNL, MXL, NL analytic delta-method standard errors. MXL, log-normal random coefficients report median WTP package’s shifted log-normal parameterization; random price coefficients rejected gof() — goodness fit: McFadden pseudo R-squared (plain adjusted) -sample hit rate, "equal_shares" (default) "market_shares" null models; now also shown summary() footer predict(..., newdata = ) — counterfactual prediction three models, either long data.frame fit-time format modified-design list (X, alt_idx, M, …); works even keep_data = FALSE. NL fits now store nest_idx top-level support logsum() — expected maximum utility (inclusive value) per choice situation MNL (closed form), MXL (simulated dedicated per-draw kernel, avoiding Jensen bias averaging utilities first), NL (nested inclusive-value formula) consumer_surplus() — expected consumer surplus logsum / (-alpha) (Train 2009, Ch. 3) delta-method standard error mean CS MNL; supports newdata policy ΔCS analysis
